data.text: I’m reading the bead context and the recent execution artifacts first so I can ground the report in concrete failures instead of guessing. After that I’ll draft the doc in the allowed `docs/helix/06-iterate/` area, verify it against the acceptance criteria, run the repo gate, and commit once.

.ddx/beads.jsonl:1:{"acceptance":"1. cli/internal/bead/axon/ exists with schema.graphql, query documents, genqlient config, and generated Go bindings.
2. TestAxonClient_SchemaBindingsCompile is added in cli/internal/bead/axon/ and exercises the generated package surface.
3. cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/axon/... passes.
4. lefthook run pre-commit passes.","created_at":"2026-05-05T02:41:06.667153148Z","description":"PROBLEM
TD-030 requires a typed Axon client package, but cli/internal/bead/axon_backend.go:20-26 still describes an in-process JSONL emulation. There is no cli/internal/bead/axon/ package yet, so the pinned schema snapshot, GraphQL operation documents, and genqlient output have nowhere to live.

ROOT CAUSE
cli/internal/bead/axon_backend.go:20-26 keeps the backend on the local JSONL-shaped emulation path, which means the repo currently has no package boundary for schema.graphql, query documents, or generated Go bindings. This leaves the TD-030 GraphQL-only client requirement unimplemented.

PROPOSED FIX
- Create cli/internal/bead/axon/schema.graphql as the pinned introspection snapshot for the Axon contract.
- Add query/mutation documents under cli/internal/bead/axon/queries/ for the bead CRUD surface needed by the package.
- Add genqlient config and generated Go bindings under cli/internal/bead/axon/ so the client can be compiled locally.
- Add a compile-focused test that instantiates the generated client surface and proves the bindings match the local package types.

NON-SCOPE
- WebSocket subscription transport.
- Replacing backend_axon.go with the wire implementation.
- Axon server-side schema or resolver work.

.ddx/beads.jsonl:2:{"acceptance":"1. cli/internal/bead/axon/ contains a websocket subscription helper for changeEvents.
2. TestAxonSubscription_ChangeEventsStream is added in cli/internal/bead/axon/ and receives ordered events from the helper.
3. The transport is wired through the package helper used by the test, not left as dead code.
4. cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/axon/... passes.
5. lefthook run pre-commit passes.","created_at":"2026-05-05T02:41:11.755929131Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":".execute-bead-wt-.execute-bead-wt-ddx-8d747049-20260505T023636-7669921a-b6bc9398-20260505T024017-db84a9cd-f2dae5c7","depends_on_id":".execute-bead-wt-.execute-bead-wt-ddx-8d747049-20260505T023636-7669921a-b6bc9398-20260505T024017-db84a9cd-a9e640e8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-05T02:41:18Z"}],"description":"PROBLEM
TD-030 requires a GraphQL subscription client for Axon changeEvents, but cli/internal/bead/axon_backend.go:20-26 still only documents a local JSONL emulation. There is no transport helper in cli/internal/bead/axon/ to open a live subscription stream against Axons WebSocket endpoint.

ROOT CAUSE
cli/internal/bead/axon_backend.go:20-26 keeps the backend in-process, so the package currently has no websocket transport boundary where GraphQL subscription setup, event decoding, and stream lifecycle management can live.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add a small subscription transport in cli/internal/bead/axon/ that opens a GraphQL WebSocket connection and subscribes to changeEvents.
- Define a narrow stream helper and event decoding path in the same package so the transport stays isolated from backend_axon.go.
- Add a focused subscription test that uses the helper to receive ordered changeEvents from a test endpoint.

NON-SCOPE
- Schema snapshot and genqlient binding generation.
- Replacing backend_axon.go with a production wire-path implementation.
- Axon server-side subscription schema work.

.ddx/beads.jsonl:3:{"acceptance":"- [ ] WorkerManager starts a supervisor goroutine on first worker launch
- [ ] Watchdog reaps workers past `WatchdogDeadline` with no phase transition in `StallDeadline`
- [ ] SIGTERM→SIGKILL escalation works on an intentionally-wedged test worker (unit test with a goroutine that ignores ctx)
- [ ] Reaped worker's bead claim is released (tracker status returns to open or claim-expired)
- [ ] `bead.reaped` event recorded on tracker with reason="watchdog" and duration
- [ ] Watchdog deadlines are configurable via ddx server config","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-18T05:48:25Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"1833233","closing_commit_sha":"13cef95535aea82c1f00dd596704f141ed131af7","created_at":"2026-04-18T04:43:00.09109294Z","description":"## Residual risk after RC1–RC4 from ddx-0a651925

Even with context propagation (RC1), wall-clock bounds (RC2), tightened breakers (RC3), and provider deadlines (RC4), a goroutine-level deadlock or panic-swallowed handler could still leave a worker alive past any reasonable bound. Today, `server.WorkerManager.Stop` (workers.go:648) only calls `handle.cancel()` — it has no escalation to OS-level signal and no autonomous trigger. ddx-b808df39 adds the *operator* tool; this bead adds the *autonomous* watchdog.

## Scope

1. WorkerManager runs a supervisor goroutine that, every minute, inspects each `workerHandle`.
2. If `time.Since(record.StartedAt) > WatchdogDeadline` (default e.g. 6h) AND `record.CurrentAttempt` has not transitioned in `StallDeadline` (default e.g. 1h), escalate:
   - Send SIGTERM to the worker's process group (requires surfacing PID, aligned with ddx-b808df39).
   - After 30s grace, send SIGKILL.
   - Mark record.State = "reaped"; update claimed bead state so the claim is not leaked.
3. Emit a `bead.reaped` event on the tracker with the full timeline so post-mortems are easy.

## Non-goals

- Per-bead runtime tuning; defaults are enough.
- Replacing ddx-b808df39 — this is defense-in-depth, not duplication. ddx-b808df39 is operator-initiated; this bead is system-initiated.

## Dependencies

- ddx-b808df39 surfaces PID in `ddx agent workers --json` output; this bead reuses that Provider call returns within deadline with a distinct error","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-18T05:30:50Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"1833233","closing_commit_sha":"b0a4fce4796e6841c9a9686ebeee197fb22a2fa7","created_at":"2026-04-18T04:42:38.821147268Z","description":"## Root cause RC4 from ddx-0a651925

After RC1 is fixed (caller ctx propagates), provider HTTP calls still need per-request deadlines. A stalled TCP socket that delivers headers and then stops sending body bytes — particularly during streaming token reads — can hang a goroutine for days.

Grepping the `DocumentDrivenDX/agent@v0.3.14` module cache shows short timeouts only on discovery probes (`provider/openai/discovery.go:46,174`, `openai.go:123`). The main `Run` call path inside agent providers appears to inherit whatever ctx DDx passes — which today is `context.Background()` (see RC1).

## Scope

1. Audit `openai`, `anthropic`, `virtual` providers in the agent library for per-request deadline usage.
2. Install a per-request deadline (default ~10-15 min) on each provider call; separate from the wall-clock bound in RC2.
3. For streaming bodies, enforce an **idle-read limit** on the response body so a mid-stream stall cannot hold a goroutine indefinitely (e.g. `http.Transport.ResponseHeaderTimeout` + periodic `SetReadDeadline` on the underlying conn).
4. Surface provider-timeout failures as a distinct status in `ExecuteBeadResult.Detail`.

## Non-goals

- Rewriting providers; minimally invasive change only.
- Changing DDx-side timeout defaults (RC2 covers outer bound).

## Prerequisite

Sibling RC1 bead must be resolved first, otherwise adding deadlines inside providers is useless while DDx still feeds them .ddx/beads.jsonl:5:{"acceptance":"- [ ] `RunOptions` accepts a caller ctx, or `Runner.Run`/`Runner.RunAgent` accept ctx explicitly
- [ ] The internal cancel is derived from the caller ctx (no `context.Background()` seeds inside Run/RunAgent)
- [ ] `agent.ExecuteBead` takes ctx and passes it down
- [ ] `server.WorkerManager.Stop(id)` cancels the running agent provider call within 2s in a unit test (fake provider blocks on ctx)
- [ ] Regression test: runner with a canceled ctx returns promptly (<500ms) regardless of mocked provider latency","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-18T04:51:41Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"1833233","closing_commit_sha":"9b044bcebc981533d2d48d4ffd79a91743dee884","created_at":"2026-04-18T04:41:24.464808955Z","description":"## Root cause RC1 from ddx-0a651925

`cli/internal/agent/runner.go:239` (Runner.Run) and `cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go:163` (Runner.RunAgent) both call `context.WithCancel(context.Background())`, discarding any context from the caller. `RunOptions` exposes no ctx field. `agent.ExecuteBead` likewise takes no ctx, so `server.WorkerManager.runWorker`'s ctx never reaches the agent call.

As a result, `WorkerManager.Stop(id)` (server/workers.go:648) cancels only the worker goroutine; the embedded `agentlib.Run` call (and any subprocess it launched) keeps running until SIGKILL. This is the structural defect behind 33–142h hung workers.

## Scope

1. Add `Context context.Context` to `RunOptions` (or change signatures to `Run(ctx, opts)`). Default to `context.Background()` when nil.
2. Derive the internal ctx from the caller's rather than `context.Background()`.
3. Thread ctx through `agent.ExecuteBead` → `runner.Run` → `agentlib.Run` → provider HTTP.
4. Update `server.WorkerManager.runWorker`'s `singleTierAttempt` closure to pass `ctx` into `agent.ExecuteBead`.

## Non-goals

- New wall-clock timeout logic (covered by sibling RC2 bead)
- Operator-facing stop command (covered by ddx-b808df39)

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01ULHZxdBTyh4d5yevDYDzq9","type":"tool_result","content":"diff --git a/cli/internal/agent/runner_context_test.go b/cli/internal/agent/runner_context_test.go\nnew file mode 100644\nindex 00000000..4a47e35d\n--- /dev/null\n+++ b/cli/internal/agent/runner_context_test.go\n@@ -0,0 +1,138 @@\n+package agent\n+\n+import (\n+\t\"context\"\n+\t\"testing\"\n+\t\"time\"\n+\n+\tagentlib \"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/agent\"\n+\t\"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert\"\n+\t\"github.com/stretchr/testify/require\"\n+)\n+\n+// TestRunAgentCancelledContextReturnsPromptly verifies that when the caller\n+// supplies an already-canceled context via RunOptions.Context, RunAgent\n+// returns within 500ms regardless of how long the provider would otherwise\n+// block. Regression anchor for ddx-0a651925 RC1: runner.Run and\n+// Runner.RunAgent previously discarded the caller's ctx by constructing\n+// their own context.WithCancel(context.Background()), so WorkerManager.Stop\n+// could not cancel the in-flight agent call.\n+func TestRunAgentCancelledContextReturnsPromptly(t *testing.T) {\n+\tprovider := &sleepProvider{\n+\t\tdelay: 30 * time.Second, // far larger than the 500ms AC deadline\n+\t\tresponse: agentlib.Response{\n+\t\t\tContent: \"never-returned\",\n+\t\t\tModel:   \"test-model\",\n+\t\t},\n+\t}\n+\n+\tr := NewRunner(Config{SessionLogDir: t.TempDir()})\n+\tr.LookPath = mockLookPath\n+\tr.AgentProvider = provider\n+\n+\tctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())\n+\tcancel() // pre-cancel so the call returns on entry\n+\n+\tstart := time.Now()\n+\tresult, err := r.RunAgent(RunOptions{\n+\t\tHarness: \"agent\",\n+\t\tPrompt:  \"test\",\n+\t\tContext: ctx,\n+\t})\n+\telapsed := time.Since(start)\n+\n+\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n+\trequire.NotNil(t, result)\n+\tassert.Equal(t, 1, result.ExitCode, \"canceled run should report non-zero exit\")\n+\tassert.NotEmpty(t, result.Error, \"canceled run should carry error text\")\n+\tassert.Less(t, elapsed, 500*time.Millisecond,\n+\t\t\"canceled ctx must abort the provider call promptly; took %v\", elapsed)\n+}\n+\n+// TestRunAgentContextCancelledMidRunReturnsPromptly cancels the caller's\n+// context while the provider is blocked, then asserts that the runner\n+// unwinds within 500ms of cancellation. This exercises the propagation\n+// path (caller ctx → internal WithCancel(parent) → agentlib.Run → provider\n+// Chat) rather than the short-circuit pre-canceled case.\n+func TestRunAgentContextCancelledMidRunReturnsPromptly(t *testing.T) {\n+\tprovider := &sleepProvider{\n+\t\tdelay: 30 * time.Second,\n+\t\tresponse: agentlib.Response{\n+\t\t\tContent: \"never-returned\",\n+\t\t\tModel:   \"test-model\",\n+\t\t},\n+\t}\n+\n+\tr := NewRunner(Config{SessionLogDir: t.TempDir()})\n+\tr.LookPath = mockLookPath\n+\tr.AgentProvider = provider\n+\n+\tctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())\n+\t// Cancel after a brief warmup so we measure the cancel→return latency.\n+\tgo func() {\n+\t\ttime.Sleep(50 * time.Millisecond)\n+\t\tcancel()\n+\t}()\n+\n+\tstart := time.Now()\n+\tresult, err := r.RunAgent(RunOptions{\n+\t\tHarness: \"agent\",\n+\t\tPrompt:  \"test\",\n+\t\tContext: ctx,\n+\t})\n+\telapsed := time.Since(start)\n+\n+\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n+\trequire.NotNil(t, result)\n+\tassert.Equal(t, 1, result.ExitCode)\n+\tassert.Less(t, elapsed, 500*time.Millisecond,\n+\t\t\"caller cancel must propagate within 500ms; took %v\", elapsed)\n+}\n+\n+// TestRunCancelledContextReturnsPromptlySubprocess verifies the same\n+// cancellation contract for the subprocess dispatch path through Runner.Run.\n+// The OSExecutor honours ctx by sending SIGKILL on ctx.Done, so a canceled\n+// ctx must not leave the runner blocked on the child exit.\n+func TestRunCancelledContextReturnsPromptlySubprocess(t *testing.T) {\n+\t// Use the mockExecutor (in-process fake) with a blocking delay. - [ ] Result.json `detail` clearly reports the time-based breaker when it fires","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-19T22:40:19Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"721226","closing_commit_sha":"7c69dc3ec37cdc89ddbaf2ce961001d451a32304","created_at":"2026-04-18T04:41:53.916108666Z","description":"## Root cause RC3 from ddx-0a651925

`cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go:218-241` trips at `stuckThreshold = maxConsecutiveCompactionFailures` (50 by default). Observed on bundle `20260416T230316-efbf91c5`: the breaker took **2h 8m** to fire (7,720,908 ms). If each iteration takes ~3 min to emit a no-op compaction event, 50 events = ~2.5h. The counter is useful but fires far too late to be a hang-guard.

## Scope

Either:
1. **Time-bounded** — fire after N no-op events **within M minutes** (e.g. 10 within 15m), OR
2. **Threshold lowered** — drop default from 50 to ~10 (only for agent harness where one iteration is already expensive), OR
3. **Wall-clock guard layered on top** — independent breaker fires after e.g. 20 min of consecutive no-op compaction events regardless of count.

Recommend option 1 or 3: time-based thresholds are more robust to provider-speed variance.

## Non-goals

- Changing the compaction algorithm itself.
- Extending breakers outside the compaction path.","events":[{"actor":"ddx","body":"{"resolved_provider":"agent","resolved_model":"qwen3.5-27b","fallback_chain":[]}","created_at":"2026-04-19T22:40:28.230027915Z","kind":"routing","source":"ddx agent execute-bead","summary":"provider=agent model=qwen3.5-27b"},{"actor":"ddx","body":"tier=cheap harness=agent model=qwen3.5-27b probe=ok

- [ ] Wall-clock timeout fires even when stdout/stderr/agent events are flowing (unit test: fake provider emits 1 event/sec for > deadline, expect cancel at deadline ± 1s)
- [ ] Idle timeout behaviour preserved (unit test: no activity for idle timeout → fire)
- [ ] Result.json distinguishes `timeout: wall-clock` from `timeout: idle`
- [ ] Documented in types.go (DefaultWallClockMS constant with comment)","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-18T05:15:25Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"1833233","closing_commit_sha":"fff2c86240b98cd687a9dc9879926b17411db3f5","created_at":"2026-04-18T04:41:40.15942901Z","description":"## Root cause RC2 from ddx-0a651925

The `DefaultTimeoutMS = 7200000` (2h) in `cli/internal/agent/types.go:363` is advertised as a hard cap but implemented as an *idle timeout*:

- `cli/internal/agent/executor.go:154-178` resets `time.NewTimer(idleTimeout)` on every stdout/stderr byte.
- `cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go:264-285` resets on every non-compaction agentlib event.

A provider that emits any heartbeat, retry line, or periodic event defeats the timer indefinitely — enabling the 33–142h hangs observed on agent projects.

Only the claude streaming path (`cli/internal/agent/claude_stream.go:373-384`) uses a true wall-clock deadline via `time.After(timeout)`. That path is why bundle `20260415T015231-c2ffa9ba` fired at exactly 2h 0m 0.098s — and nothing else does.

## Scope

1. Add an absolute wall-clock deadline in addition to (not replacing) the idle timeout.
2. Default the wall-clock bound to something operators can trust — e.g. 3x `DefaultTimeoutMS` or a new `DefaultWallClockMS`.
3. Apply in: `OSExecutor.ExecuteInDir` (subprocess path) and `Runner.RunAgent` (embedded agent path).
4. Reflect the wall-clock error in result.json as a distinct `error: "wall-clock deadline exceeded after Xh"` so it is not confused with idle timeout.

## Non-goals

- Lowering or removing the existing idle timeout.
- Per-provider tuning (can be 5. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.","created_at":"2026-05-05T11:37:14.708641486Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":".execute-bead-wt-ddx-0e5c3005-20260505T113434-3317d134-7fb75345","depends_on_id":"ddx-d6730314","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-06T03:30:30Z"}],"description":"PROBLEM
Prompt-injection boundary markers are not yet explicit around artifact bodies that later flow into prompt assembly. The current server-side artifact readers in `cli/internal/server/state_executions.go:205-217` clamp text for transport, but they do not wrap the body in an explicit untrusted-data envelope for downstream prompt renderers. The review prompt path in `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1561-1630` only uses XML tags for the bead envelope; it does not mark artifact content with a dedicated boundary contract.

ROOT CAUSE
There is no shared delimiter helper for untrusted artifact bodies. The current prompt builders rely on ad hoc XML wrappers and transport clamping, which are not the same thing as a prompt-injection boundary that clearly distinguishes trusted instructions from untrusted artifact text.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add a small helper (likely in `cli/internal/evidence` or the prompt-rendering package that owns the assembler) that wraps artifact bodies in a canonical untrusted-data delimiter pair.
- Update the prompt-rendering call sites that inline artifact bodies to use the wrapper before interpolation.
- Add a regression test named `TestArtifactBodyDelimitedAsUntrusted` that asserts the delimiter pair appears around the artifact payload and that the raw body is still preserved inside the wrapper.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not alter the transport-level clamp behavior or its `truncated` metadata.
- Do not change review verdict parsing or the FEAT-022 telemetry schema in this slice.
- Do not rewrite unrelated server text egress paths.","events":[{"actor":"ddx","body":"{"resolved_provider":"codex","resolved_model":"gpt-5.4-mini","fallback_chain":[],"actual_power":8}","created_at":"2026-05-05T14:44:37.174355984Z","kind":"routing","source":"ddx agent execute-bead","summary":"provider=codex model=gpt-5.4-mini"},{"actor":"ddx","body":"{"attempt_id":"20260505T143454-88ed54dc","harness":"codex","model":"gpt-5.4-mini","input_tokens":4861400,"output_tokens":17293,"total_tokens":4878693,"cost_usd":0,"duration_ms":580211,"exit_code":0}","created_at":"2026-05-05T14:44:37.425832645Z","kind":"cost","source":"ddx agent execute-bead","summary":"tokens=4878693 model=gpt-5.4-mini"},{"actor":"ddx","body":"{"escalation_count":0,"fallback_chain":[],"final_tier":"","requested_profile":"","requested_tier":"","resolved_model":"gpt-5.4-mini","resolved_provider":"codex","resolved_tier":""}","created_at":"2026-05-05T14:44:44.068530101Z","kind":"routing","source":"ddx agent execute-loop","summary":"provider=codex model=gpt-5.4-mini"},{"actor":"erik","body":"failure_class=review-error: transport

The current review/session prompt path does not assemble a turn-structured prompt envelope with pinned evidence, rolling session memory, and explicit refusal on minimum-floor overflow. `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go:204-333` only builds a single-shot review prompt around bead metadata, governing docs, and diff. `cli/internal/server/review_session.go:22-224` stores artifact identity, template refs, prompt refs, and append-only turns, but there is no renderer that turns that session state into a structured prompt with pinned + rolling sections.

ROOT CAUSE
`cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go:204-333` is the current bounded review assembler. It clamps governing docs and the diff, then emits one prompt blob and telemetry, but it does not model per-turn pinned/rolling sections or a dedicated refusal class for a minimum pinned floor that cannot fit. The review-session store in `cli/internal/server/review_session.go:22-224` persists the right inputs (`artifact_git_rev`, `template_ref`, `prompt_ref`, `turns.jsonl`) yet nothing consumes them in a structured prompt-rendering step.

PROPOSED FIX
- `cli/internal/server/review_session.go` or a new helper in the same package: add a prompt renderer that emits pinned sections for system rubric, artifact identity, Story 17 template+prompt, first-user-intent, and explicit user decisions, followed by rolling sections for session memory summary, last K verbatim turns, and unresolved findings.
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go`: thread the structured renderer into the review path, keep FEAT-022 byte accounting on the assembled prompt, and short-circuit before provider dispatch when the minimum pinned floor does not fit. The refusal should surface `PROMPT_BUDGET_EXCEEDED` with the observed bytes and cap in the error text.
- Add tests named `TestReviewSessionPromptAssemblyPinnedRolling`, `TestReviewSessionPromptAssemblyBudgetRefusal`, and `TestReviewPromptTelemetryIncludesSectionBytes` to pin the section order, refusal behavior, and byte accounting.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change the existing review verdict parser or the review error taxonomy.
- Do not add new server text-egress caps in this slice.
- Do not rework the existing diff clamping primitives; this bead only consumes them.","events_attachment":".execute-bead-wt-ddx-0e5c3005-20260505T113434-3317d134-f25a64d9/events.jsonl","execute-loop-no-changes-count":1,"id":".execute-bead-wt-ddx-0e5c3005-20260505T113434-3317d134-f25a64d9","issue_type":"task","labels":["phase:2","area:agent","kind:feature","story:18"],"parent":"ddx-0e5c3005","priority":2,"session_id":"eb-d9ae6d84","spec-id":"FEAT-022","status":"closed","title":"agent: implement structured review prompt assembly with budget refusal and telemetry","updated_at":"2026-05-05T20:07:36.061587634Z"}

The work described by this bead's AC was implemented and merged in commit
e188d9d3 ("feat(agent): wire triage dispatch for
lock_contention/execution_failed/no_changes [...]"), which is an ancestor of
the current execute-bead base 26d80c83. That commit added 755 lines across
three files:

  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_status.go    (+3)
  cli/internal/agent/triage_dispatch.go        (+405)
  cli/internal/agent/triage_dispatch_test.go   (+347)

AC mapping (all satisfied by code already present in the worktree):

  AC#1 — Lock-contention classification + retry-with-backoff with ≥3 steps
    before escalation:
    - IsLockContentionError (triage_dispatch.go) matches .git/index.lock,
      "another git process seems to be running", staging-tracker / tracker
      lock messages.
    - ClassifyAttemptForTriage routes execution_failed reports whose Detail
      matches IsLockContentionError to triage.FailureModeLockContention.
    - RunWithLockBackoff retries an AttemptFunc against a backoff schedule;
      LockContentionBackoff defaults to {30s, 90s, 300s} (three slots),
      sets Exhausted=true after consuming all slots so callers escalate.
    - DefaultPolicy.Ladders[FailureModeLockContention] =
      {retry_with_backoff, retry_with_backoff, needs_human} so cross-pass
      lock_contention also escalates.

  AC#2 — execution_failed enters triage and produces escalate_tier or
    needs_human depending on attempt count:
    - DefaultPolicy.Ladders[FailureModeExecutionFailed] =
      {re_attempt_with_context, escalate_tier, needs_human}.
    - DispatchPostAttempt records a triage-decision event per attempt;
      HistoryFromEvents recovers the prior-mode count so the ladder
      advances per occurrence (verified by
      TestDispatchExecutionFailedEscalates).

  AC#3 — no_changes with rationale not citing AC items files a clarification
    follow-up and closes the original:
    - RationaleCitesAC returns false on vague rationales and true when the
      rationale (a) is "specific" via rationaleIsSpecific (cites SHA, test
      name, file path) or (b) shares ≥2 AC content tokens of length ≥6.
    - DispatchPostAttempt, on ActionFileFollowup for FailureModeNoChanges,
      calls fileNoChangesFollowup which creates a child bead inheriting
      labels/spec-id, adds kind:clarification, embeds the worker's
      rationale and parent AC, and back-links via the
      triage_followup_for / triage_followup_reason Extra fields. Then
      CloseWithEvidence closes the parent (BaseRev as closing SHA).

  AC#4 — Tests cover all four scenarios:
    - TestRunWithLockBackoffSucceedsOnRetry — lock-error retry success
    - TestRunWithLockBackoffExhausts — backoff exhaustion across all three
      schedule slots
    - TestDispatchExecutionFailedEscalates — full execution_failed ladder
      (re_attempt → escalate_tier → needs_human, then clamps)
    - TestDispatchNoChangesFilesFollowup — weak-rationale no_changes files
      a clarification child and closes the parent
    Plus TestIsLockContentionError, TestClassifyAttemptForTriage,
    TestRationaleCitesAC, TestRunWithLockBackoffPropagatesNonLockError,
    TestRunWithLockBackoffRetriesOnAttemptError,
    TestDispatchNoChangesStrongRationaleSkipsTriage as supporting cases.

Verification:
  cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/ -run \
    'TestRunWithLockBackoff|TestDispatch|TestClassifyAttemptForTriage|TestIsLockContentionError|TestRationaleCitesAC' \
    -count=1
  → ok  github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/agent  0.024s

No code changes made. Blocking dependency not yet resolved.

## What is done

Investigation complete. The existing server code has:
- `scanExecutionBundles()` / `GetExecutionsGraphQL()` in `state_executions.go` — loads `.ddx/executions/<attempt-id>/` bundles as execution records
- `/api/exec/runs` routes and `ddx_exec_history`/`ddx_exec_run` MCP tools for definition-based exec runs (`.ddx/exec/` store)
- GraphQL `executions` query already exposes the layer-2 try records

The `/api/tries` routes and `ddx_list_tries`/`ddx_show_try` MCP tools could be implemented against the existing `.ddx/executions/` storage — those bundles are precisely the layer-2 try records.

## What is blocking

**Bead #18 (`ddx-0910d431`, "architecture sketch: three-layer run substrate") is still open.**

The bead description says: "Gate on bead #18 substrate unification."

Without the architecture sketch, the following are undefined:

1. **Unified record shape** — the plan says `.ddx/exec-runs/` and `.ddx/executions/<attempt-id>/` collapse into one record shape with layer metadata. Field names, nesting, and serialization format are unspecified.

2. **Layer-3 work records** (`/api/runs`) — queue drain / execute-loop session records. `.ddx/exec-runs/` does not exist on disk. There is no on-disk storage for layer-3 records; workers are in-memory only. The bead #18 sketch must define where and how these are persisted.

3. **Layer metadata fields** — the `layer` discriminant (values: 1/2/3? "run"/"try"/"work"?) and any additional layer-specific metadata fields are undefined until bead #18 is written.

4. **`/api/runs/{id}/tries` and `/api/tries/{id}/runs`** — the cross-references require both a stable run-id concept (missing) and layer-1 records separate from their parent try (which do not currently exist).

5. **`ddx_list_runs` and `ddx_show_run`** — no underlying data to serve.

Implementing only `/api/tries` (2 of 4 route pairs, 2 of 4 MCP tools) would be a partial bead. A partial implementation would also risk field-name conflicts when bead #18 closes and defines the real unified shape.

## What a follow-up attempt needs

1. Close bead #18 (`ddx-0910d431`) first. The architecture sketch must define:
   - Unified record shape (all layers use the same struct + `layer` discriminant)
   - On-disk path for layer-3 records (e.g., `.ddx/exec-runs/<session-id>/`)
   - Layer-3 record fields: session-id, bead IDs attempted, start/end, outcome summary
   - Layer-1 record fields: how individual agent runs are stored within a try bundle (separate file vs sub-directory)

2. With the spec in hand, implement:
   - `/api/tries` → `scanExecutionBundles()` with `layer=2` response field
   - `/api/tries/{id}` → bundle detail (already exists as `GetExecutionGraphQL()`)
   - `/api/tries/{id}/runs` → layer-1 records within the try bundle (once defined by #18)
   - `/api/runs` → scan layer-3 records from the new on-disk path
   - `/api/runs/{id}` → show one work session
   - `/api/runs/{id}/tries` → layer-2 records grouped by run session ID
   - MCP tools: `ddx_list_tries`, `ddx_show_try`, `ddx_list_runs`, `ddx_show_run`

.ddx/beads.jsonl:25:{"acceptance":"1. EITHER: closed as 'covered by tool-calling gating' with a written justification (e.g., test or comment in the tool-calling bead's deliverables that asserts SupportsTools => SupportsStructuredOutput for all currently routed providers).
2. OR: SupportsStructuredOutput tri-state added to ModelEntry/Surface + capabilities table, with rejection logic in evaluateCandidate, RouteRequest.RequiresStructuredOutput field, and unit tests parallel to the tool-calling bead's tests.
3. go test ./cli/... is green (only applies to outcome 2).","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","closing_commit_sha":"5328120be55ab6af07a5cd35c7dc7f7a22f30f96","created_at":"2026-04-18T06:50:54.195936927Z","description":"## Lowest-priority sibling of ddx-4817edfd.

Some provider/model combos don't implement JSON-mode / response_format=json_object / tool-use-required. If a bead prompt depends on structured output, today routing's blindness means the failure surfaces at dispatch.

Per the parent bead's analysis, this is probably covered incidentally by the tool-calling gating sub-bead — most reasoning-tool-capable models also support structured output. This bead exists to either (a) explicitly add a SupportsStructuredOutput capability, or (b) close as 'covered by tool-calling gating' once that bead lands and we verify the assumption.

## Decision gate (do this first)

Before writing code, verify the 'covered by tool-calling' hypothesis:
- Survey the providers we currently route to (anthropic, openai, openrouter, omlx, lmstudio, ollama) and check whether each one's tool-calling support implies JSON-mode support.
- If yes everywhere we care about: close this bead with a note in the tool-calling bead's tests / docs that asserts the implication.
- If no (any counter-example): proceed with scope below.

## Scope (only if decision gate fails)

- Add SupportsStructuredOutput tri-state to ModelEntry / Surface and to capabilities.go's curated table.
- RouteRequest.RequiresStructuredOutput bool; execute-bead sets it when the prompt requires JSON mode.
- evaluateCandidate rejects RequiresStructuredOutput=true on SupportsStructuredOutput=no. Unknown passes with -v warning.

## Files (only if decision gate fails)

- cli/internal/agent/types.go
- cli/internal/agent/routing.go
- cli/internal/agent/catalog.go
- cli/internal/agent/capabilities.go","id":".execute-bead-wt-ddx-4817edfd-20260418T064927-78e29296-22cadb21","issue_type":"task","labels":["ddx","phase:build","kind:implementation","area:agent","area:routing"],"notes":"Superseded by agent-1a486c2e — single routing engine in agent/internal/routing","parent":"ddx-4817edfd","priority":4,"status":"closed","title":"routing: structured-output (JSON-mode) gating — likely incidental to tool-calling","updated_at":"2026-04-18T22:43:47.191941677Z"}
.ddx/beads.jsonl:26:{"acceptance":"1. ModelEntry / Surface in catalog.go gain SupportsTools (tri-state: yes / no / unknown).
2. capabilities.go (new file) ships a curated default table for known harness+flavor pairs.
3. Discovery code populates SupportsTools from /v1/models/status when the field is present in the response.
4. RouteRequest.RequiresTools added; execute-bead sets it true for bead execution.
5. evaluateCandidate rejects RequiresTools=true candidates known to be SupportsTools=no, with a debug reason.
6. Unknown SupportsTools does not reject; logs at -v.
7. Unit tests cover: known-no rejection, known-yes pass, unknown pass with warning, RequiresTools=false disables the check.
8. go test ./cli/... is green.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","closing_commit_sha":"5328120be55ab6af07a5cd35c7dc7f7a22f30f96","created_at":"2026-04-18T06:50:30.504525579Z","description":"## Reject routes that can't actually run tool-calling work (sibling of ddx-4817edfd).

Some omlx flavors and older local OpenAI-compat servers do not implement /v1/chat/completions with tools. Today routing fuzzy-matches them and dispatch fails with 'tools not supported' — or worse, silently returns a no-tool response that the execute-bead loop cannot consume.

## Scope

- Per-provider / per-flavor capability metadata: SupportsTools bool. Two layers:
  1. Hand-curated table for known harnesses (claude, codex, opus, etc.) and provider flavors (openai, anthropic, openrouter, omlx, lmstudio, ollama).
  2. Discovery-driven enrichment when /v1/models/status (or equivalent) advertises tool support per model.
- RouteRequest gains RequiresTools bool (default false to preserve current behavior; execute-bead sets true).
- evaluateCandidate rejects candidates where RequiresTools=true and the resolved (provider, flavor, model) combo is known NOT to support tools. Unknown = pass with -v warning, same pattern as context-window bead.
- Capability metadata location: cli/internal/agent/catalog.go on ModelEntry/Surface, plus a curated table in cli/internal/agent/capabilities.go (or co-located).

## Out of scope

- Context-window, structured-output, effort, permissions gating (sibling beads).
- Probing servers actively at routing time (uses cached discovery + table).

## Files

- cli/internal/agent/types.go (RouteRequest.RequiresTools, capability fields on CandidatePlan)
- cli/internal/agent/routing.go (rejection in rejectIfNotViable / evaluateCandidate)
- cli/internal/agent/catalog.go (SupportsTools on ModelEntry / Surface)
- cli/internal/agent/capabilities.go (NEW — curated table)","id":".execute-bead-wt-ddx-4817edfd-20260418T064927-78e29296-854cb8a7","issue_type":"task","labels":["ddx","phase:build","kind:implementation","area:agent","area:routing","area:catalog"],"notes":"Superseded by agent-1a486c2e — single routing engine in agent/internal/routing","parent":"ddx-4817edfd","priority":2,"status":"closed","title":"routing: tool-calling support gating with per-flavor capability metadata","updated_at":"2026-04-18T22:43:47.285105492Z"}
.ddx/beads.jsonl:27:{"acceptance":"1. Capability table tracks SupportsEffort and SupportsPermission per (provider, model_pattern).
2. evaluateCandidate rejects candidates that don't honor a RouteRequest's --effort or --permissions, with a debug reason that names which capability and which provider/model.
3. Unknown capability does NOT reject; logs at -v.
4. Unit tests cover: known-no-effort rejection, known-yes pass, unknown pass with warning, no-effort-requested disables the check (parallel structure for permissions).
5. go test ./cli/... is green.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","closing_commit_sha":"5328120be55ab6af07a5cd35c7dc7f7a22f30f96","created_at":"2026-04-18T06:50:41.86041297Z","description":"## Provider-specific honoring of --effort and --permissions (sibling of ddx-4817edfd).

Today, Harness.EffortFlag is checked at routing.go:rejectIfNotViable, so harnesses without effort support are rejected when --effort is set. But provider-level gating is missing: e.g., openrouter doesn't honor --effort on non-reasoning models, and --permissions unrestricted may not apply to every harness+provider combination. Routing should reject or downgrade rather than dispatch and silently no-op the flag.

## Scope

- Add effort/permissions capability fields to the curated capability table (same file added by the tool-calling sub-bead, or co-locate here if that one ships first).
  - SupportsEffort tri-state per (provider, model).
  - SupportsPermission(permission_class) — at minimum 'unrestricted'.
- evaluateCandidate / rejectIfNotViable: when RouteRequest names an --effort or --permissions value not honored by the resolved (provider, model) combo, reject with a clear reason. Unknown = pass with -v warning.
- Document expected behavior for each known provider in capabilities.go comments (openrouter non-reasoning models = no effort, etc.).

## Out of scope

- Context-window, tool-calling, structured-output gating (sibling beads).
- Building a 'downgrade' path (e.g., silently strip --effort and continue). Rejection is sufficient for v1; downgrade is a follow-up.

## Files

- cli/internal/agent/routing.go
- cli/internal/agent/capabilities.go (shared with tool-calling bead — or new file if order differs)
- cli/internal/agent/types.go","id":".execute-bead-wt-ddx-4817edfd-20260418T064927-78e29296-bb230ad5","issue_type":"task","labels":["ddx","phase:build","kind:implementation","area:agent","area:routing"],"notes":"Superseded by agent-1a486c2e — single routing engine in agent/internal/routing","parent":"ddx-4817edfd","priority":3,"status":"closed","title":"routing: effort/permissions gating per provider+model (not just per harness)","updated_at":"2026-04-18T22:43:47.3806568Z"}
.ddx/beads.jsonl:28:{"acceptance":"1. RouteRequest has EstimatedPromptTokens uint32 field, zero value means 'unspecified'.
2. evaluateCandidate rejects candidates whose known context window < EstimatedPromptTokens; emits a routing-debug reason that names the model and its window.
3. Unknown context window does NOT cause rejection; logs a warning at -v.
4. execute-bead populates EstimatedPromptTokens with at least a char/4 estimate of the assembled prompt.
5. Unit test in routing_test.go covers: (a) below-window rejection, (b) above-window pass, (c) unknown-window pass with warning, (d) EstimatedPromptTokens=0 disables the check.
6. go test ./cli/... is green.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","closing_commit_sha":"5328120be55ab6af07a5cd35c7dc7f7a22f30f96","created_at":"2026-04-18T06:50:16.477597182Z","description":"## Smallest useful slice of capability gating (sibling of ddx-4817edfd).

Routing currently does not check whether the resolved model's context window can hold the bead prompt. A 32k-context fuzzy match against an 80k-token bead prompt routes 'successfully' and fails mid-dispatch with a provider 400 or truncated output.

## Scope

- Add optional EstimatedPromptTokens (uint32) to RouteRequest (cli/internal/agent/types.go).
- In evaluateCandidate (cli/internal/agent/routing.go), when EstimatedPromptTokens is set and > 0, reject (or penalize) candidates whose concrete_model context window is below it.
- Source of context window per candidate: v0.3.11's LookupModelLimits first, then catalog ModelEntry.ContextWindow (cli/internal/agent/catalog.go) as fallback. If neither is known, treat as 'unknown' — do NOT reject (avoid false negatives), but log at -v.
- Caller side: pass EstimatedPromptTokens from execute-bead when the bead prompt is assembled (best-effort — can use char/4 heuristic if a tokenizer is unavailable).

## Out of scope

- Tool-calling, structured-output, effort, permissions gating (separate sibling beads).
- Adding a tokenizer dependency. char/4 estimate is fine for v1.

## Files

- cli/internal/agent/types.go
- cli/internal/agent/routing.go
- cli/internal/agent/catalog.go
- cli/cmd/agent_execute_bead.go (or wherever the bead prompt is assembled)","id":".execute-bead-wt-ddx-4817edfd-20260418T064927-78e29296-f5cec42a","issue_type":"task","labels":["ddx","phase:build","kind:implementation","area:agent","area:routing"],"notes":"Superseded by agent-1a486c2e — single routing engine in agent/internal/routing","parent":"ddx-4817edfd","priority":2,"status":"closed","title":"routing: context-window gating in evaluateCandidate","updated_at":"2026-04-18T22:43:47.481306052Z"}

rationale: status: already_complete

The bead's implementation was committed as 83ec7853 ("feat(server): reportedWorkers GraphQL query exposes worker_ingest derived view") in a prior execution attempt and merged into main via 185ab68e. The current base-rev (5fe31689) already contains the full implementation:

  - cli/internal/server/graphql/schema.graphql           (reportedWorkers query + ReportedWorker/WorkerFreshness types)
  - cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_reported_workers.go  (resolver reading s.workerIngest snapshot)
  - cli/internal/server/graphql/generated.go             (regenerated gqlgen)
  - cli/internal/server/graphql/models.go                (ReportedWorker + WorkerFreshness models)
  - cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver.go              (resolver wiring)
  - cli/internal/server/reported_workers_adapter.go      (snapshot -> model adapter using freshnessState)
  - cli/internal/server/worker_ingest.go                 (snapshot accessor)
  - cli/internal/server/server.go                        (workerIngest registry handle)
  - cli/internal/server/graphql_reported_workers_test.go (TestGraphQL_Workers_FreshnessFields with connected/stale/disconnected sub-tests + TestGraphQL_Workers_MultipleWorkersPerProject)

The earlier REVIEW:BLOCK note was raised against an attempt whose diff contained only execution-evidence files; the actual feature commit (83ec7853) had already been merged ahead of that review and the BLOCK is stale. All AC items are satisfied in HEAD and the targeted tests pass.

The current chaos harness already names jsonl and axon in cli/internal/bead/chaos_test.go:23-36, but the rest of the package still relies on ad hoc store construction and has no package-wide backend matrix to prove the new Axon path behaves identically.

ROOT CAUSE
cli/internal/bead/chaos_test.go:23-36 only defines the backend table; there is no end-to-end package test matrix yet that runs the conformance suite through both backends and keeps the existing package tests honest. The rest of the test package still defaults to newTestStore(t) in many places, so the Axon path is not yet a first-class test target.

PROPOSED FIX
- Extend the backend test helpers so the package conformance suite can run under both jsonl and axon.
- Keep TestChaos_ConcurrentAppendSafety, TestChaos_AtomicStatusTransitions, TestChaos_ConcurrentCloseAndAppend, and TestChaos_ConcurrentCloseNotLost as explicit backend matrix coverage.
- Make the package test harness clearly fail if either backend diverges.

NON-SCOPE
- GraphQL client generation.
- Backend selection/config plumbing.
- Subscription integration smoke tests.","id":".execute-bead-wt-ddx-8d747049-20260505T023636-7669921a-1a576b9d","issue_type":"task","labels":["phase:2"," area:beads"," area:storage"," kind:feature"," blocked-on-upstream:axon-05c1019d"," blocked-on-upstream:axon-82b6f7b2"],"notes":"Superseded by ddx-958b8fc3. The current Axon backend does not talk to Axon at all; it rewrites local JSONL snapshots under <dir>/axon/ instead of using the GraphQL entity store described by TD-030. That is visible in cli/internal/bead/axon_backend.go:59-215, where AxonBackend stores beads/events as local files and serializes them with writeAtomicFile instead of remote operations.

ROOT CAUSE
cli/internal/bead/axon_backend.go:59-215 implements the backend as file-backed emulation, not a GraphQL-backed store. This bypasses the typed client, bypasses optimistic concurrency against Axon, and makes the backend selectable only in a fake local mode.

PROPOSED FIX
- Replace local snapshot writes with real GraphQL CRUD, dependency, and event mutations using cli/internal/bead/axon/.
- Preserve the two-collection model from TD-030: ddx_beads and ddx_bead_events, with event_of links.
- Keep the existing backend interface surface working for Create, Get, Update, Claim, Close, DepAdd, DepRemove, AppendEvent, ExportTo, and Import.
- Preserve jsonl as the default backend path so day-1 behavior does not change.

NON-SCOPE
- Backend selection config plumbing.
- Package test matrix broadening.
- WebSocket subscription smoke tests.","id":".execute-bead-wt-ddx-8d747049-20260505T023636-7669921a-26f338ad","issue_type":"task","labels":["phase:2"," area:beads"," area:storage"," kind:feature"," blocked-on-upstream:axon-05c1019d"," blocked-on-upstream:axon-82b6f7b2"],"notes":"Superseded by ddx-9c5bca8f. .ddx/beads.jsonl:46:{"acceptance":"1. cli/internal/server/workers.go:runWorker exec's the resolved ddx binary (os.Executable() with DDX_EXECUTABLE override) with subcommand 'work' and a flag-vector derived from ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec. No in-process call to worker.Run remains in this code path.
2. WorkerRecord.PID is populated with the subprocess PID before the exec returns to StartExecuteLoop; signal-based Stop and watchdog reaping continue to work against the child PID (TestStopExecuteLoopWorker_KillsSubprocess covers SIGTERM→SIGKILL grace).
3. Progress events from the child reach the parent's drainProgress goroutine via the existing /api/workers/<id>/event ingestion path (server.go:884). At least one new test TestRunWorker_ProgressBridge_FromChild asserts that an event POSTed by the child appears on a subscribed SSE channel.
4. BeadWorkerFactory is deleted from WorkerManager. workers_test.go/workers_e2e_test.go/workers_stop_propagation_test.go/workers_route_preflight_test.go/workers_config_test.go/graphql_adapters_default_profile_test.go updated: tests that depended on injecting an in-process executor are rewritten using the test-reentry pattern (ddx-work-stub) or deleted with rationale comment if the semantics moved into cli/cmd/* tests.
5. cd cli && go test ./internal/server/... ./cmd/... green; lefthook run pre-commit passes.","created_at":"2026-05-03T22:59:39.685160971Z","description":"PROBLEM: cli/internal/server/workers.go:runWorker runs the bead-execution loop in-process, knitting together LandCoordinator, DefaultBeadReviewer, CostCapTracker, RoutePreflight, ExecuteBeadWorker, ProgressCh→drainProgress, WakeCh, BeadWorkerFactory injection, and watchdog phase-stall tracking — all of which contradict ADR-022's autonomous-default model where workers are subprocesses identical to operator-launched 'ddx work'.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE: cli/internal/server/workers.go:658-940 (runWorker) builds an in-process executor closure (singleTierAttempt at lines 679-747), wraps it with a CostCap tracker (lines 753-797), wires a DefaultBeadReviewer (lines 801-809), and calls worker.Run(ctx, rcfg, ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime{...}). All of this duplicates work that 'ddx work' (cli/cmd/work.go → cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:runAgentExecuteLoopImpl) already does, just inside the parent server process.

PROPOSED FIX: Replace the in-process worker.Run(...) call with exec.CommandContext(ctx, dddxBin, 'work', <flags>) where dddxBin = os.Executable() (override-able via DDX_EXECUTABLE env for tests). Project root passed via --project flag or DDX_PROJECT_ROOT env. Provider/harness/model/profile/effort/poll-interval/once/no-review/review-harness/review-model/label-filter/model-ref translated to existing ddx work flags. Capture child PID into WorkerRecord.PID (already a field at workers.go:147). Stop() and the watchdog already signal via PID (workers.go:1070, 1279) — verify the cross-process path works; SIGTERM with kill grace then SIGKILL.

Progress event ingestion changes: child process emits via workerprobe to /api/workers/<id>/event (this endpoint exists, server.go:884). The parent's WorkerManager subscribes to those events (in-memory derived view from the workers ingestion handler) and forwards into the per-subprocess workerHandle.progressCh so existing SSE subscribers and drainProgress logic continue to work. Mapping: parent records the WorkerRecord.id at exec time; child registers with /api/workers/register and gets a probe-issued worker_id; parent correlates by ProjectRoot + ExecutorPID + StartedAt window.

Drop in-process LandCoordinator/Reviewer/CostCap/RoutePreflight wiring inside runWorker (they now run inside the child). LandCoordinators registry stays for the existing inline 'ddx work' path (cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1714).

WakeCh: replace with SIGUSR1 to the child (or document as no-op for the v1 of this refactor; WakeProject still walks workers but only sends to live in-process handles — kept null-safe for subprocess workers).

BeadWorkerFactory: removed. Tests that previously injected a fake executor migrate to either (a) reentry-stub mode where the test binary acts as 'ddx work' via go test re-exec pattern, or (b) deletion as no-longer-applicable (semantics now live in cli/cmd/* tests).

NON-SCOPE: handleStartExecuteLoopWorker rewrite (covered by 7b). Deletion of ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec struct (covered by 7b). End-to-end real-claude integration (covered by 7c). UI/GraphQL surface changes (already covered by ADR-022 step 5).","id":".execute-bead-wt-ddx-eccc6efb-20260503T225413-91120fa0-5be5f460","issue_type":"task","labels":["phase:2","area:server","kind:refactor","adr:022"],"parent":"ddx-eccc6efb","priority":2,"status":"closed","title":"ADR-022 step 7a: workers.go runWorker exec's ddx work subprocess (parent-side plumbing)","updated_at":"2026-05-03T23:00:18.503933642Z"}

Large beads must be decomposed without operator intervention before implementation, or immediately after a failed implementation attempt proves the pre-claim gate missed a too-large bead. The recent bounced workflow beads showed the gap: the implementer correctly reported that the bead was too large, but decomposition was prohibited inside the attempted worktree/depth context, so an operator had to split it manually.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1386 embeds decomposition instructions in the implementer prompt, which happens after dispatch rather than before claim.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1661 emits an implementation-attempt decomposition depth cap message; that prompt cap can prevent the worker from creating children even when the queue-level orchestrator could still split safely.
- docs/triage/decomposition.md requires ac_map and parent blocking semantics, but execute-loop does not yet file children from a pre-claim intake decision or from post-attempt no_changes_needs_investigation caused by oversized work.
- cli/internal/agent/try/no_changes.go:29 defines no_changes_needs_investigation, but the work loop does not yet convert a too-large rationale into orchestrator-level decomposition.

PROPOSED FIX
- Handle pre-claim too_large_decomposed by creating child beads with standalone descriptions/AC and dependency wiring.
- Handle post-attempt no_changes_needs_investigation whose rationale says the bead is too large, too broad, depth-capped inside the attempt, or requires decomposition by invoking the same orchestrator splitter on the original bead.
- Treat implementation-attempt depth as advisory only; queue-level max_decomposition_depth is the limit that decides whether DDx can split or must ask the operator.
- Parent receives triage-decomposed event body with child_ids, rationale, and ac_map.
- Every parent AC must map to at least one child AC or explicit needs_human/non_scope; lossy decomposition blocks for operator instead of creating children.
- Parent is blocked/dependency-blocked on children and not claimed again for implementation.

NON-SCOPE
- Safe rewrite handling.
- Review-time decomposition beyond using the same splitter when review_too_large is already classified.","events":[{"actor":"ddx","body":"{"resolved_provider":"codex","resolved_model":"gpt-5.4-mini","fallback_chain":[],"actual_power":8}","created_at":"2026-05-06T07:14:46.730021942Z","kind":"routing","source":"ddx agent execute-bead","summary":"provider=codex model=gpt-5.4-mini"},{"actor":"ddx","body":"{"attempt_id":"20260506T071425-bd3e30f7","harness":"codex","model":"gpt-5.4-mini","input_tokens":59940,"output_tokens":862,"total_tokens":60802,"cost_usd":0,"duration_ms":17777,"exit_code":0}","created_at":"2026-05-06T07:14:46.947554809Z","kind":"cost","source":"ddx agent execute-bead","summary":"tokens=60802 model=gpt-5.4-mini"},{"actor":"erik","body":"The bead is still too large to execute safely in a single pass, but this worktree is already at decomposition depth 3. .ddx/beads.jsonl:51:{"acceptance":"1. execute-loop attempts 3-way auto-resolution of preserved iteration onto current tip before discarding (ort strategy + sane heuristics). 2. On 3-way failure, execute-loop dispatches a focused conflict-resolve agent run (cheap tier) with the conflict files + AC + iteration commit message; on success, commits the merge. 3. Only after (1) and (2) fail does the loop park the bead. Park outcome is kind:land-conflict-unresolvable (or kind:land-conflict-needs-human if the focused-resolve agent escalated to BLOCKING). 4. Cooldown for land-conflict-unresolvable is 5-30 min, capped under the 24h cap from ddx-a458af7c. 5. Same code path applies to execution_failed when the agent produced a commit before failing (timeout-with-preserved-commit case). 6. Regression tests: (a) mechanical conflict auto-resolves, (b) structural conflict escalates to focused-resolve, (c) timeout-with-preserved-commit attempts the merge. 7. CHANGELOG entry. 8. Update FEAT-006 (or wherever the execute-loop outcome contract is documented) to list the new structured outcomes.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","closing_commit_sha":"ee729c4aed85c64e3f19a5f76d2addabd815918b","created_at":"2026-04-29T18:16:41.722078707Z","description":"When ddx agent execute-bead lands a commit locally but merging into the base branch produces conflicts, the loop today preserves the result at refs/ddx/iterations/<bead>/<ts>-<base> and unclaims the bead with status: land_conflict. The bead reopens; subsequent attempts re-execute from scratch — burning more agent time and dollars even though the original work is intact in the preserved ref.

OBSERVED 2026-04-29 on ddx-52469547 (FEAT-015 bead 3, 'runtime fallback removal + audit-side migration'):
- Sonnet ran for ~3h, produced commit 12fbf6dd1b98 covering all 7 AC items.
- The base branch advanced significantly during the run (V1, V2, V3 vision beads + dogfood + concurrent work).
- The run hit the agent's 3h hard timeout right at the test phase.
- execute-loop preserved at refs/ddx/iterations/ddx-52469547/20260429T175646Z-aa7b31785a44 with status execution_failed (timeout, but functionally identical to land_conflict for the merge story).
- Operator attempted git merge --no-ff 12fbf6dd, hit 7 file-level conflicts (mechanical drift from parallel beads), aborted, and only avoided wasting the work because a parallel attempt (263b5eff) had landed the exact same scope cleanly. That parallel landing is a coincidence, not a guarantee.

The general case: a preserved iteration represents real money spent and real work done. land_conflict / execution_failed-with-result that bails to 'reopen and re-run' throws that work away. The loop should at least attempt to reuse the preserved result.

REQUESTED BEHAVIOR

1. On land_conflict (merge produces conflicts when applying the iteration onto current tip), execute-loop should:
   a. Try a 3-way merge of the preserved iteration ref onto current main using merge.conflictStyle=diff3 and ort strategy with -X ours/-X theirs heuristics. Only if the merge resolves cleanly under those heuristics, commit it as a non-fast-forward merge with a message like 'Merge preserved iteration ddx-<id> after base drift'.
   b. If 3-way auto-resolution fails, dispatch a SMALL focused agent run (cheap tier) with the conflict files, the preserved iteration's commit message, and the bead AC. Its sole job: resolve the conflicts, verify build/tests, commit. Do NOT re-do the bead from scratch.
   c. Only if (a) and (b) both fail, fall back to today's behavior (preserve, unclaim, set short cooldown, mark for human review with a structured outcome distinct from generic execution_failed — perhaps kind:land-conflict-unresolvable).

2. Cooldown for land_conflict-unresolvable should be short (5-30 min), not the 24h cap, since the conflict will resolve faster as soon as a human or another bead lands a clarifying change.

3. Same logic should apply to execution_failed-with-preserved-result (timeouts mid-merge): if a commit was produced before the failure, attempt to merge it before discarding the run.

4. Reuse the auto-recover plumbing landed for push race in ddx-a458af7c (landPushAutoRecover, capLoopCooldown). Specifically the conflict-vs-clean-recovery branching should mirror landPushAutoRecover's structure.

5. Regression tests:
   - Fake git that produces a mechanical conflict resolvable via -X ours: assert auto-resolution succeeds and bead lands.
   - Fake git that produces an irreconcilable structural conflict: assert escalation to focused-resolve agent (or, at minimum, that today's behavior is taken with the new structured outcome).
   - Preserved-iteration-on-timeout case: assert the preserved commit IS attempted as a merge before falling back.

ACCEPTANCE OF FAILURE: if the focused-resolve agent itself fails or escalates to BLOCKING, that's a structured outcome (kind:land-conflict-needs-human) — the bead is parked with the conflict context and preserved ref clearly recorded.

Related: ddx-a458af7c (push race auto-recover, this session's predecessor work).","events":[{"actor":"ddx","body":"{"escalation_count":0,"fallback_chain":[],"final_tier":"","requested_profile":"","requested_tier":"","resolved_model":"sonnet","resolved_provider":"claude","resolved_tier":""}","created_at":"2026-04-29T18:47:14.013496692Z","kind":"routing","source":"ddx agent execute-loop","summary":"provider=claude model=sonnet"},{"actor":"erik","body":"Diff at rev 1550c5382a is a bare merge commit with no code changes; none of the 8 AC items (3-way auto-resolve, focused-resolve agent dispatch, structured outcomes, cooldown cap, timeout-with-preserved-commit path, regression tests, CHANGELOG, FEAT-006 update) can be evaluated as implemented.

(a) What is done
----------------
AC #1 — 3-way auto-resolution of preserved iteration onto current tip (ort
strategy + -X ours heuristic) before discarding:
  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:845 landConflictAutoRecover(...)
  Uses `git merge --no-ff -s ort -X ours` in a temp worktree at currentTip,
  aborts cleanly on failure, advances refs/heads/<target> via update-ref +
  SyncWorkTreeToHead on success.

AC #2 — On 3-way failure, dispatch a focused conflict-resolve agent:
  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:279 ExecuteBeadWorker.ConflictResolver
  func(ctx, beadID, preserveRef, projectRoot) (newTip string, isBlocking bool, err error)
  Invoked at execute_bead_loop.go:1450 inside runConflictRecovery; on success
  the merge is committed and the bead lands.

AC #3 — Park outcome distinguishes unresolvable vs needs-human:
  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_status.go:264 ExecuteBeadStatusLandConflictUnresolvable
  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_status.go:270 ExecuteBeadStatusLandConflictNeedsHuman
  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1467 sets NeedsHuman when
    ConflictResolver returns isBlocking=true; else Unresolvable.
  Events emitted: kind:land-conflict-unresolvable / kind:land-conflict-needs-human
  (execute_bead_loop.go:1490-1501).

AC #4 — Cooldown 5-30 min, capped under the 24h push-race cap:
  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:64
    const LandConflictCooldown = 15 * time.Minute
  Applied at execute_bead_loop.go:824 (parkUntil = now + LandConflictCooldown);
  capLoopCooldown still bounds the value under 24h.

AC #5 — Same code path runs for execution_failed-with-preserved-commit (timeout):
  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1397 shouldAttemptConflictRecovery
  Returns true for ExecuteBeadStatusLandConflict OR
  ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed when BaseRev != ResultRev and a PreserveRef
  exists. Verified by TestExecuteBeadLoopExecutionFailed_WithPreservedCommit_AttemptsRecovery.

AC #6 — Regression tests (all pass under `go test ./internal/agent/`):

2. ParseReviewVerdict accepts: raw JSON; ```json …``` fenced JSON; mixed prose+JSON where the JSON is the last fenced block. Rejects: unknown verdict values; missing verdict field; truncated JSON.
3. cd cli && go test -run TestParseReviewVerdict ./internal/agent/... passes with one subtest per accepted/rejected case above.
4. cd cli && go test -run TestReviewerEmitsJSONContract ./internal/agent/... passes: uses a fixture reviewer transcript with '### Verdict: APPROVE' wrapped markdown and asserts the extractor call site rejects it with the 'unparseable' class — confirming the markdown path is gone.
5. cd cli && go test -run TestReviewerJSONHappyPath ./internal/agent/... passes: a fixture transcript containing the JSON contract parses to a ReviewVerdict with verdict=APPROVE.
6. The reviewer prompt template (in execute_bead_review.go or its asset file) requests JSON-only output and includes a schema example; cd cli && go test -run TestReviewPromptRequestsJSON ./internal/agent/... passes by string-matching the template.
7. The previously-observed regression — extractor parsing 'BLOCK' from the options header line — has a regression test: cd cli && go test -run TestReviewerNoOptionsHeaderRegression ./internal/agent/... feeds a transcript that includes only the prompt template's options header echoed back and asserts the parser rejects it (unparseable), not parses it as BLOCK.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-25T09:24:10Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"196235","closing_commit_sha":"ed2219bf951ce9b7770ed684fb6c789a51b3344d","created_at":"2026-04-25T08:00:51.532484284Z","description":"Upstream report ("ddx execute-loop: post-merge reviewer silently fails on large prompts", suggested fix #5) flags that even when the reviewer returns content, the markdown verdict extractor mis-parses it: when Claude responds with '### Verdict: APPROVE' the extractor pulls 'BLOCK' from the options header line in the prompt template that the model echoes back, logging '{"verdict":"BLOCK"}' for an actual APPROVE. This silently lets regressions land.

FEAT-022 (parent ddx-0c35470e) is scoped to prompt assembly and size invariants; response parsing is out of scope there. File this as a sibling.

Switch the reviewer contract to a machine-readable JSON object emitted as the model's final response:

  {
    "verdict": "APPROVE" | "REQUEST_CHANGES" | "BLOCK",
    "findings": [
      { "severity": "info"|"warn"|"block", "summary": "…", "location": "path:line?" }
    ],
    "summary": "≤300 char human-readable verdict justification"
  }

Replace the existing markdown extractor with a strict JSON parser:
1. Update the reviewer prompt template (post-collapse: agent.BuildReviewPrompt) to instruct the model to emit ONLY a JSON object matching the schema, fenced as ```json … ``` for robustness against models that won't emit raw JSON.
2. Add internal/agent/review_verdict.go with a typed ReviewVerdict struct and a ParseReviewVerdict([]byte) (ReviewVerdict, error) that strips an optional ```json fence and decodes; reject on unknown verdict values.
3. Replace the regex-based extractor call site in execute_bead_review.go with ParseReviewVerdict; on parse error, emit the existing 'unparseable' review-error class (introduced by ddx-70c1d2e2) — do NOT close the bead.
4. Schema-version the contract: include schema_version: 1 in the prompt; the parser tolerates unknown fields but rejects unknown verdict values.

In-scope files:
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go (prompt template, extractor call site)
- cli/internal/agent/review_verdict.go (new)
- cli/internal/agent/review_verdict_test.go (new)
- prompt template asset under cli/internal/agent/assets/ if separated

Out of scope:
- Bounded retry / error-class taxonomy (ddx-70c1d2e2 owns this; this bead emits 'unparseable' into that taxonomy)
- Prompt size invariants (FEAT-022)
- Grading verdicts (separate bead if needed; this is review-only)

Cross-reference: This bead's `unparseable` emission feeds into ddx-70c1d2e2's bounded-retry logic. ddx-70c1d2e2 should be reviewed once both land to confirm the unparseable retry budget is sane (the upstream report observed real APPROVE verdicts being lost, so unparseable should retry, not terminal-escalate immediately).","events":[{"actor":"ddx","body":"{"resolved_provider":"claude","fallback_chain":[]}","created_at":"2026-04-25T09:39:10.457997834Z","kind":"routing","source":"ddx agent execute-bead","summary":"provider=claude"},{"actor":"ddx","body":"{"attempt_id":"20260425T092410-e9991cad","harness":"claude","input_tokens":56,"output_tokens":43684,"total_tokens":43740,"cost_usd":4.689253000000002,"duration_ms":899008,"exit_code":0}","created_at":"2026-04-25T09:39:10.52615806Z","kind":"cost","source":"ddx agent execute-bead","summary":"tokens=43740 cost_usd=4.6893"},{"actor":"ddx","body":"{"escalation_count":0,"fallback_chain":[],"final_tier":"","requested_profile":"","requested_tier":"","resolved_model":"","resolved_provider":"claude","resolved_tier":""}","created_at":"2026-04-25T09:39:13.380291229Z","kind":"routing","source":"ddx agent execute-loop","summary":"provider=claude"},{"actor":"ddx","body":"APPROVE

Problem:
The central FEAT-006/FEAT-010/FEAT-001 docs, DDx skills, and operator guide now describe the MinPower/MaxPower run/try/work boundary. A broad repo scan still finds legacy command/routing vocabulary in active or semi-active docs outside that core set: token-awareness and onboarding test plans, FEAT-002/004/007/008/011/012/013/014/019/022, TD/SD design docs, and older operator/UI references. Some hits are historical alignment reviews or explicit deprecation notes and can remain, but active governing docs must not teach `ddx agent run`, `execute-bead`, `execute-loop`, profile flags, quorum flags, or DDx-side ResolveRoute as live workflow surfaces.

Why this matters:
Agents and humans search the docs, not just the latest feature file. Leaving old active docs around will reintroduce the exact mixed command/routing model this cleanup is meant to eliminate.

Required fix:
Classify broad grep hits into historical/archive/deprecation versus active governing docs. Update active docs to `ddx run` / `ddx try` / `ddx work`, MinPower/MaxPower, passthrough-only pins, and status/debug-only ResolveRoute. Add a docs grep allowlist so future references to removed command surfaces must be in explicit migration/deprecation/historical sections.

Acceptance:
1. A repo-wide docs grep for legacy public surfaces passes with only allowlisted historical/deprecation hits.
2. Active FEAT/TD/SD/test-plan docs use run/try/work and MinPower/MaxPower consistently.
3. `docs/agent-execute.md` remains the operator guide for run/try/work, not agent execute-loop/execute-bead.
4. .ddx/beads.jsonl:60:{"acceptance":"New file cli/internal/agent/script.go implements RunScript(opts) that reads a directive file, executes directives against opts.WorkDir using real filesystem and git operations, and returns a real Result. The harness is registered in cli/internal/agent/registry.go as `script` and marked always-available in Discover(). Directive grammar supports at minimum: append-line, create-file, delete-file, commit, set-exit, no-op, sleep-ms, fail-during. Environment variable interpolation supports ${DDX_BEAD_ID}, ${DDX_ATTEMPT_ID}, ${DDX_WORKER_ID}. Unit tests in cli/internal/agent/script_test.go cover: (1) append-line + commit produces a real commit with the expected content, (2) no-op produces zero commits and exit 0, (3) create-file + commit produces the file and a commit, (4) set-exit 1 makes the run return exit 1, (5) fail-during 2 executes directives 0 and 1 but errors on directive 2, (6) env var interpolation replaces ${DDX_BEAD_ID} with the value from Correlation, (7) invoking via `ddx agent run --harness=script --model=<file>` works end-to-end with a real temp git repo. go test ./internal/agent -run TestScript completes in under 5 seconds.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","created_at":"2026-04-14T14:49:12.379615428Z","description":"The execute-loop / execute-bead / land-coordinator system cannot be tested end-to-end today because every "fake" agent in the codebase returns a canned Result{} without actually writing files or making commits. `cli/internal/agent/virtual.go` is a prompt-response dictionary replayer — it returns canned stdout based on prompt hash but does not touch the filesystem or git. `fakeAgentRunner` and `fakeExecuteBeadGit` in `cli/cmd/agent_execute_bead_test.go` are in-memory mocks that track expected call counts. Neither produces actual commits that the orchestration layer can rebase, merge, or land.

The consequence: every test that claims to cover concurrency, serialization, or git orchestration uses mocked git and therefore cannot catch merge-race, rebase-conflict, or coordinator-serialization bugs. `TestConcurrentWorkersNoMergeRace` at execute_bead_orchestrator_test.go:228 is particularly misleading — it uses separate orchTestGitOps instances per worker so the shared-writer invariant is never exercised. The test passes because no shared state exists to race on.

## What this bead builds

A new deterministic `script` harness (cli/internal/agent/script.go, mirroring cli/internal/agent/virtual.go):

- Registered alongside claude/codex/agent/virtual in cli/internal/agent/registry.go
- Always available (no binary probe required)
- Takes a directive file (passed via RunOptions.Model or a new RunOptions.ScriptFile field — either works)
- Reads the directive file, executes each line against opts.WorkDir as real filesystem + git operations
- Returns a real Result{ExitCode, Output} that the loop and orchestrator can observe

## Directive grammar

Line-based, comments start with `#`. Each directive performs a real side effect:

```
append-line <path> <text>          # append text to file (create if missing)
create-file <path> <content>       # create or overwrite file
delete-file <path>                 # rm -f path
modify-line <path> <regex> <repl>  # sed-style in-place substitution
run <shell-command>                # exec in WorkDir with clean env
commit <message>                   # git add -A && git commit -m <message>
set-exit <code>                    # final exit code (default 0)
no-op                              # no commits, no files, exit 0
fail-during <index>                # synthetic failure at the Nth directive
sleep-ms <n>                       # deterministic delay (for scheduling tests)
```

Directives run in order. Environment variables DDX_BEAD_ID, DDX_ATTEMPT_ID, DDX_WORKER_ID are interpolated into text arguments so directives can be parameterized. Example:

```
append-line tests/smoke.txt "hello from ${DDX_BEAD_ID}"
commit "smoke: ${DDX_BEAD_ID}"
```

## Invocation

From the CLI: `ddx agent run --harness=script --model=./testdata/append-smoke.txt --prompt=/dev/null`

Programmatically from a test:
```go
runner := agent.NewRunner(agent.Config{Harness: "script"})
result, err := runner.Run(agent.RunOptions{
    WorkDir: wtPath,
    Model:   "testdata/append-smoke.txt",
    Correlation: map[string]string{"bead_id": beadID, "attempt_id": attemptID},
})
```

## Implementation notes

- Mirror the dispatch pattern in cli/internal/agent/runner.go:81 where `virtual` is intercepted
- Always-available in Discover() same as virtual + agent
- No external dependencies beyond what the agent package already uses (os, os/exec for git)
- Estimated size: ~200 lines of implementation + ~200 lines of tests
- Unit tests in cli/internal/agent/script_test.go must prove each directive actually performs its claimed side effect on a real temp git repo

## What this enables

Every test case in beads B (integration tests) and C (server runWorker regression test) depends on this harness existing. Without it, the land-coordinator redesign ships behind whatever real claude happens to do on the day the test runs.","id":"ddx-025046ac","issue_type":"task","labels":["area:agent","area:testing","kind:infrastructure","phase:build"],"parent":"ddx-8746d8a6","priority":0,"session_id":"eb-53f5df0","status":"closed","title":"Implement deterministic script harness for orchestration stress testing","updated_at":"2026-04-14T15:00:05.850093133Z"}

(a) WHAT IS DONE
----------------
The previous attempt (commit 5ae02964) migrated the high-value `r.Run(RunOptions{...})`
and `RunViaService(..., RunOptions{...})` test sites onto the SD-024 successor API
(`r.RunWithConfig` + `RunWithConfigViaService` + `AgentRunRuntime`) across:

  - cli/internal/agent/agent_test.go    (~22 sites: TestRunWith*, TestSessionLogging,
                                         TestRunPromptFile, TestRunStdinMode,
                                         TestRunModel*, TestRunNonZeroExit,
                                         TestRunUnknownHarness, TestRunEmptyPrompt,
                                         TestIntegration_CodexEcho, TestRunOpencode*,
                                         and the *Permissions* pair)
  - cli/internal/agent/runner_test.go   (lmstudio / openrouter dispatch regressions)
  - cli/internal/agent/script_test.go   (runScript helper now builds rcfg via
                                         TestRunConfigOpts and dispatches via
                                         RunWithConfigViaService)

Those 26 sites are the bulk of the "test sites that exercise the high-level agent
run path" called out in the bead description.

(b) WHAT IS BLOCKING
--------------------
The bead AC reads:

    rg 'RunOptions\{|CompareOptions\{|QuorumOptions\{' cli/ returns 0 matches outside
    the type definitions about to be retired.

This is a *global* gate. It cannot be satisfied by test-only changes — it requires
retiring every remaining `RunOptions{...}` construction in both production and test
code. The remaining sites fall into two classes:

CLASS A — tests that call functions whose signature *takes* RunOptions, so the
RunOptions construction is not a "test site" the test could rewrite without
changing the function under test:

  - cli/internal/agent/agent_test.go        BuildArgs(h, RunOptions{...}, model)  — 14 sites
                                            BuildArgs is the function under test.
  - cli/internal/agent/prompt_ingress_oversize_test.go
        line 78  resolvePrompt(RunOptions{PromptFile})              — exercises r.resolvePrompt
        line 85  defaultResolvePromptForCompare(RunOptions{...})    — exercises pkg-level helper
        line 112 RunViaServiceWith(ctx, svc, dir, RunOptions{...})  — exercises stub-injection helper
  - cli/internal/agent/session_index_test.go
        line 195 SessionIndexEntryFromResult(root, RunOptions{...}, ...)

AC2. Land-coordinator runs a basic sanity gate before merging: the file must still parse for its filetype (TypeScript files compile, Svelte files have valid <script>/<template>/<style> structure if they had one previously, JSON is valid JSON, etc.). Failed parse = preserve as iteration ref, do not merge.

AC3. When a parse-fail or large-deletion gate trips, the bead is marked 'preserved-needs-review' and the operator is alerted via the bead's notes field, with a link to the iteration ref and a one-line summary of what gate fired.

AC4. Post-merge typecheck/build is run as a final gate (project-level command from .ddx/config.yaml or sensible default like 'cargo check' / 'bun run build'). If post-merge build fails, AUTOMATICALLY revert the merge and preserve the result instead. This is the safety net for cases AC2 doesn't catch.

AC5. Documentation in docs/ explains the safety gates, how to override them when intentional (e.g. for genuine large deletions), and what 'preserved-needs-review' means operationally.

AC6. Master became unbuildable: vite-plugin-svelte threw 'Expected token }' because the policies/+page.svelte file lost its <script lang="ts"> wrapper along with the entire HTML template and style sections.

The damaging merge:

  commit be1073f4333c289e48680cf8c05ceefa7ea9c075
  Merge: 0c5557b 2f35ad2
  Author: ddx-land-coordinator <coordinator@ddx.local>
  Date:   Wed Apr 29 20:56:46 2026 -0400

      Merge bead axon-80979cb8 attempt 20260430T005434- into master

       .../databases/[database]/policies/+page.svelte     | 1448 --------------------
       1 file changed, 1448 deletions(-)

The post-merge codex reviewer DID block subsequent attempts that built on the broken base ('the diff only adds execution metadata' / 'the new Playwright coverage is incorrect... the collection view never loads'), but the original 1448-line deletion went through unchecked. Five subsequent worker attempts ran on top of the now-broken master, each adding ~80 lines of partial reconstructions, none producing a valid Svelte file.

Net damage on master before manual recovery:
  ui/src/routes/.../policies/+page.svelte      1552 → 186 lines (1366 lines lost)
  ui/tests/e2e/policy-enforcement.spec.ts      1043 → 158 lines (885 lines lost)
  master state: vite-plugin-svelte fails to compile, UI bundle cannot build, docker e2e harness cannot start

Manual recovery (commit bc4b492 on axon master): git checkout HEAD~14 of both files, re-stage, commit, push. 2415 lines restored across 3 files.

Why this happened (hypothesis):
  1. The bead-attempt branch had a partial reconstruction of the file (104 lines of just imports/state, no template).
  2. The land-coordinator did a 3-way merge with master at base_rev. The base_rev was ALSO the partial reconstruction (because EARLIER attempts had already truncated it).
  3. Three-way merge produced a 'clean' delete of the 1448 lines that existed only on master's side because base_rev was already broken.

Or alternatively: the agent's tool calls deleted content from the worktree without re-emitting the full file (e.g. used Edit with overlapping replace blocks that effectively truncated), the harness committed whatever was on disk without sanity-checking magnitude, and the land-coordinator merged the partial content as authoritative.","execute-loop-heartbeat-at":"2026-04-30T12:21:44.162888426Z","id":"ddx-efe058be","issue_type":"bug","labels":["area:agent","area:execute-bead","area:land-coordinator","kind:safety","severity:critical"],"owner":"erik","priority":0,"status":"closed","title":"execute-bead: land-coordinator merged a result that deleted 1448 lines from a single file (catastrophic damage)","updated_at":"2026-04-30T12:22:05.677486788Z"}

  - resolver_operator_prompt.go:180-189 rejects when the request has no
