rationale: # No-changes rationale — ddx-fdd3ea36 (routing point release adoption)

## Decision

Stopping without a commit. This bead's acceptance criteria describe a
multi-PR release-adoption epic, not a single bounded change. Attempting it
in one execute-bead pass would produce either (a) a partial commit that
fails most AC items, or (b) a sprawling change touching dispatch, config
schema, CLI surface, frontend Playwright fixtures, and CHANGELOG/docs
without the up-front design alignment the bead itself calls for.

## What is already done (verified in this worktree)

1. **Upstream routing-plan beads are all closed.** All six beads under
   label `routing-plan` in `~/Projects/agent/.ddx/beads.jsonl` are in
   `closed` status:
     - agent-0dafc7f0 (cost-aware routing)
     - agent-191a74f9 (default + local + standard profiles)
     - agent-1f46cf22 (routing docs)
     - agent-53f38d95 (public decision trace)
     - agent-bab52778 (supported-models allow-list)
     - agent-dfabb10b (typed errors)

2. **Their closing commits are in DDx's currently-pinned upstream tag.**
   `cli/go.mod` references `github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/agent v0.9.23`.
   `git tag --contains` for each routing-plan closing commit returns
   v0.9.10 as the earliest containing tag. v0.9.23 (and v0.9.24 latest)
   transitively contain all of them. So the *binary surface* AC #1
   wants is already accessible via the pinned go.mod.

## What is NOT done — and why this can't be a one-shot pass

### AC #1 — CHANGELOG-as-released-narrative

AC #1 asks for "a ddx-agent version whose CHANGELOG documents: first-class
default and local profiles; supported-models allow-list in routing inputs +
gating; ErrHarnessModelIncompatible; public decision-trace …; cost
dimension on ProviderEntry with unknown-cost fallback."

Reading `~/Projects/agent/CHANGELOG.md`:
  - v0.9.10 documents prompt-caching work, not the routing-plan work.
  - v0.9.11 documents ADR-006 override-as-failure-signal.
  - v0.9.12-v0.9.24 cover lucebox/vllm, conformance, corpus, terminal-bench,
    pi-runner panic. None of these releases bundles a coherent
    "routing point release" narrative.

The closing commits for the six beads landed scattered between tags as
side-quest work; none of those tags' release notes call out the
combined contract DDx now wants to consume. AC #1 effectively requires
either (a) an upstream re-release that publishes a "v0.9.25 — routing
point release" entry covering exactly those items, OR (b) DDx's bead
description must be relaxed to accept "the code is in v0.9.23, even if
no single CHANGELOG block summarizes it." Either resolution is a
governance decision, not a coding task this worktree can execute.

### AC #2-7 — DDx-side surface (D1-D5)

These criteria collectively require:
  - rewriting the worker dispatch path so the default issues exactly one
    `ResolveRoute(Profile: "default")` call with no Model / no Harness /
    no Provider, removing all DDx-side ladder synthesis;
  - inserting a typed-incompatibility preflight gate before bead claim
    (`execute_bead_loop.go` ~line 341);
  - making `agent.routing.default_harness` a hard error at config load,
    `profile_ladders` and `model_overrides` opt-in via `--escalate`/`--override-model`,
    with one-time process warnings;
  - guaranteeing override flag combinations route through unchanged to
    upstream and surface upstream typed errors;
  - removing `ResolveProfileLadder` / `ResolveTierModelRef` /
    `workersByHarness` escalation helpers from the default path with
    dead-code detection in CI;
  - adding an integration test that reproduces the historical 19-burn
    drain-queue bug AND new e2e tests covering each override
    combination (claude+opus, model-only, harness-only, profile+harness
    conflict);
  - adding Playwright drain-queue golden fixtures matching the user's
    real config;
  - updating CHANGELOG and docs.

This is on the order of 1-3k LOC delta across:
  - `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go`
  - `cli/internal/agent/profile_ladder.go`
  - `cli/internal/agent/dispatch.go`
  - `cli/internal/agent/serviceconfig.go`
  - `cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go` (workerDispatchAdapter)
  - `cli/internal/config/*` (deprecated field schema)
  - `cli/cmd/agent_*.go` (override flag plumbing)
  - new tests (unit, integration, regression, Playwright)

That work spans D1-D5 in the bead's own sequencing list, which the bead
explicitly bundles into "one DDx PR" — but a PR of that size needs
architectural alignment with the user (especially around the breaking
config change in D4 and the dead-code detector in AC #7) before the
first line is written. execute-bead's contract is to satisfy the AC
deterministically, not to make architecture decisions.

### AC #8 — D6 / D7 follow-up beads

The bead requires that follow-up beads be filed:
  - one for the route-status / providers-UI decision-trace consumer,
  - acknowledgement that `ddx-05b4cc9d` (workersByProject filter)
    stays independent.

Filing beads requires `ddx bead create` invocations against the
project's tracker and a commit that mutates `.ddx/beads.jsonl`. This is
mechanical but should ride along with the substantive PR, not a no-op.

### AC #10 — drain-queue regression golden

A net-new integration test that reproduces the user's historical
19-burn failure with their real config. Designing this test
(particularly the gemini-binary-installed assertion, since CI may not
have gemini available) is itself a sub-bead.

## What a follow-up attempt needs

To make this bead landable in a single execute-bead pass, the bead
must be split into smaller beads, each of which is a well-defined PR:

1. **routing-bump** — bump `cli/go.mod` from v0.9.23 to whatever
   tag (v0.9.25?) the upstream cuts as the routing point release; add
   DDx CHANGELOG entry citing upstream release notes. Trivially
   satisfies AC #1 once upstream cuts the tag.

2. **routing-default-path** — D1 + D2 only. Worker dispatch + default
   `ddx agent run` and `ddx work` issue `ResolveRoute(Profile:
   "default")`. New unit tests. Does not touch config schema yet.

3. **routing-preflight-gate** — D3 only. Insert preflight before
   `Claim` at `execute_bead_loop.go:~341`. Test with seeded
   incompatibility.

4. **routing-config-deprecation** — D4 only. Hard error on
   `default_harness`, opt-in warnings on others. Migration test +
   CHANGELOG breaking-change note.

5. **routing-overrides-e2e** — D5 + AC #5 e2e tests for each override
   combination.

6. **routing-drain-regression** — AC #10. Standalone regression test
   that reproduces the historical 19-burn bug.

7. **routing-cleanup** — AC #7 dead-code detector + removal of
   compensating logic.

8. **routing-frontend-goldens** — AC #6 Playwright fixtures.

9. **routing-followups** — AC #8 file the route-status / providers-UI
   bead.

Each of those is a clean execute-bead pass with provable AC. The
current bead's AC list is the union of all nine — too broad for one
attempt.

## Recommendation to the orchestrator

Reopen this bead with `helix-evolve` (or equivalent) to split it into
the nine sub-beads above, then queue them in order. The first three
(bump + default-path + preflight-gate) unblock the user's stated
goals (G1/G2/G3) in the smallest possible PR.

The reduced-scope language in the bead's own description ("DDx-side
tracker only", "blocks on a single upstream point release") suggests
this bead was meant as the EPIC, not the deliverable. Treating it as
the deliverable in execute-bead is what produced this no-op outcome.

rationale: # No-changes rationale — ddx-fdd3ea36 (routing point release adoption)

This is the second consecutive no_changes for this bead (prior attempt
20260428T222708-3b6ff345 reached the same conclusion). I re-verified the
upstream / DDx state and concur with that prior analysis: the bead is a
multi-PR epic, not a single bounded execute-bead pass, and it should be
split before another implementation attempt.

## What is verified done

1. All six upstream routing-plan beads in `~/Projects/agent/.ddx/` are
   `closed`:
     agent-0dafc7f0, agent-191a74f9, agent-1f46cf22,
     agent-53f38d95, agent-bab52778, agent-dfabb10b.

2. Their closing commits are reachable from the tag DDx already pins:
   `cli/go.mod` → `github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/agent v0.9.23`.
   `git merge-base --is-ancestor <SHA> v0.9.23` succeeds for the
   closing commits of every routing-plan bead checked
   (db26108, b645280, 0bc2bf4, b85e90e). So the upstream binary
   surface AC #1 wants is already on the consumer's classpath; no
   `go.mod` bump is even required.

## Why this still cannot be a single-pass commit

The bead's AC enumerates 10 items spanning D1–D5 plus follow-ups:

  - D1+D2: rip out DDx-side tier-ladder synthesis from the default
    `ddx agent run` / `ddx work` / `ddx agent execute-bead` paths so
    each issues exactly one `ResolveRoute(Profile: "default")` call —
    touches `workerDispatchAdapter`, `execute_bead_loop.go`,
    `agent_cmd.go`, `profile_ladder.go`, `serviceconfig.go`.
  - D3: insert a typed-incompatibility preflight gate before
    `beadStore.Claim` at `execute_bead_loop.go:~352`.
  - D4: deliberate breaking config change — `default_harness` becomes
    a hard error at load; `profile_ladders` and `model_overrides`
    move to opt-in-only with one-time warnings, requires schema
    deletion + migration test + CHANGELOG breaking-change note.
  - D5: CLI override surface — flag plumbing + per-flag e2e tests
    covering harness-only, model-only, harness+model conflict, and
    profile+harness conflict.
  - AC #6: Playwright drain-queue golden matching the user's real
    endpoint config + a "no gemini + non-gemini-model fallback"
    assertion.
  - AC #7: search-and-delete pass for any DDx-side
    allow-list / exact-pin / cost-scoring re-implementation, plus a
    dead-code detector wired into CI to catch orphans of
    `ResolveProfileLadder` / `ResolveTierModelRef`.
  - AC #8: file follow-up beads for D6 (route-status / providers UI)
    and reaffirm `ddx-05b4cc9d` independence.
  - AC #9: link DDx CLAUDE.md / AGENTS.md to upstream docs and write
    DDx CHANGELOG entry.
  - AC #10: net-new regression integration test that reproduces the
    19-burn drain-queue failure mode with the user's exact config —
    requires fixture work (gemini binary presence, minimax override)
    that is itself a sub-bead.

Conservatively: 1–3k LOC across ~10 Go files plus frontend Playwright
fixtures plus CI wiring plus tracker mutations. Crucially D4 is a
deliberate breaking change to user-facing config schema; landing it
without explicit user alignment risks breaking real `.ddx.yml` files
in the wild. The bead description itself describes the work as "one
DDx PR" but the AC list is the union of nine independent
deliverables.

execute-bead's contract is to satisfy AC deterministically, not to
make architecture or breaking-change decisions across nine
sub-deliverables in one pass. A partial commit would fail most AC
items; a sprawling commit would couple breaking schema changes to
mechanical refactors and frontend fixtures, which is exactly the
shape of PR the bead's own sequencing tries to avoid.

## What a follow-up attempt needs

Split this bead into the nine smaller beads listed in the prior
attempt's rationale (`.ddx/executions/20260428T222708-3b6ff345/result.json`,
section "What a follow-up attempt needs"):

  1. routing-bump (trivial; AC #1 — though arguably already
     satisfied by v0.9.23)
  2. routing-default-path (D1 + D2)
  3. routing-preflight-gate (D3)
  4. routing-config-deprecation (D4 — breaking, needs explicit
     user sign-off in its own bead description)
  5. routing-overrides-e2e (D5 + AC #5)
  6. routing-drain-regression (AC #10)
  7. routing-cleanup (AC #7 dead-code detector)
  8. routing-frontend-goldens (AC #6)
  9. routing-followups (AC #8)

Recommendation: reopen this bead with helix-evolve to perform the
split, keep ddx-fdd3ea36 as the epic tracker, and queue beads 2 and 3
first — they unblock G1/G2/G3 with the smallest reasonable PR.

The reduced-scope language in the bead's own description ("DDx-side
tracker only", "blocks on a single upstream point release") confirms
this was meant as an EPIC, not a deliverable. Two consecutive
execute-bead passes have now reached the same conclusion; further
re-execution at higher model tiers will not change the outcome —
the blocker is bead shape, not capability.

      agent-1f46cf22 (routing reference docs)
      agent-53f38d95 (public RouteDecision.Candidates trace)
      agent-bab52778 (supported-models allow-list)
      agent-dfabb10b (typed ErrHarnessModelIncompatible / ErrProfilePinConflict)
  * The needed public types are present in the pinned module at
    /home/erik/go/pkg/mod/github.com/!document!driven!d!x/agent@v0.9.23/routing_errors.go:
      ErrHarnessModelIncompatible, ErrProfilePinConflict, ErrNoProfileCandidate,
      ErrUnknownProfile, ErrNoLiveProvider, DecisionWithCandidates interface
      with RouteCandidates() []RouteCandidate.

(b) WHAT IS BLOCKING THIS ATTEMPT
=================================
The bead is an epic-tracker, not a single deliverable. AC #2 through #10
collectively require — in one PR with no compensating paths retained — all of:

  D1. workerDispatchAdapter.DispatchWorker rewrite + Drain-button parity.
  D2. Removal of default-path tier-ladder synthesis from
      ddx agent run / ddx agent execute-bead / ddx work; ResolveProfileLadder
      and ResolveTierModelRef gated behind --escalate only.
  D3. Preflight gate inserted before beadStore.Claim in execute_bead_loop.go
      (the bead points at line 352, which has already drifted — the actual
      Claim site is now line 341; the loop is 1052 lines and has substantial
      surrounding dispatch state to thread through).
  D4. Hard-error breaking change for `agent.routing.default_harness`;
      one-time warnings + default-path ignore for `profile_ladders` and
      `model_overrides`. Schema deletion in
      cli/internal/config/schema/config.schema.json. Config-load test fixtures.
  D5. CLI surface parity for ddx agent run (no flags / --profile / --harness /
      --model / combos), ddx work, ddx agent execute-bead.

  Plus tests demanded by the AC:
  - AC #2 integration test reproducing the original 19-burn drain failure
    against the user's real config shape.
  - AC #3 worker-level failure record assertion with seeded pathological route
    via an upstream test seam that does not currently exist on the DDx side.
  - AC #5 four end-to-end override-behavior tests (incl. cost-tiebreak
    seeded-providers test).
  - AC #6 Playwright golden for the drain-queue UI path
    (cli/internal/server/frontend); fixture must mirror user's real
    endpoint config.
  - AC #7 dead-code detector wired into CI catching orphaned ladder helpers.
  - AC #10 regression-guard integration test that fails before this work and
    succeeds after.

  Plus the directive in AC #7 — "Search DDx for code that re-implements any
  of: allow-list checks, exact-pin filtering, profile-to-preference mapping,
  provider cost scoring. Must be absent. If found, PR fails review." —
  requires a deliberate audit + removal pass spanning at least:
      cli/internal/agent/harness_config.go
      cli/internal/agent/profile_ladder.go
      cli/internal/agent/profile_ladder_test.go
      cli/internal/config/config.go
      cli/internal/config/types.go
      cli/internal/config/routing_ladder_test.go
      cli/internal/config/schema/config.schema.json
      cli/internal/server/workers.go
      cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go
      cli/cmd/agent_run_profile_test.go
      cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go (1052 lines)
      cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_test.go
      cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_runwithconfig_test.go
      cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_resolvers_e2e_test.go
      cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_pin_propagation_test.go

This is a multi-thousand-line, multi-subsystem refactor with a hard
breaking-change config migration and a new Playwright golden. It cannot be
landed responsibly in a single execute-bead pass without a much narrower
sub-bead breakdown. Attempting it as one commit risks: half-finished tier-
ladder removal that leaves the default path in an undefined state; a config
schema break that hard-errors existing user configs without the migration
warnings actually firing; or merging a Playwright golden whose fixture
silently passes because the underlying dispatch path was not fully rewired.

The previous attempt already correctly scoped this bead to AC #1 only and
documented the upstream prerequisite. Doing more here without
decomposition would either produce a bad commit or violate
DOWITYTD on a bead whose own description acknowledges it is a
"DDx-side tracker only" for an upstream-driven epic.

(c) WHAT A FOLLOW-UP ATTEMPT NEEDS
==================================
Decompose ddx-fdd3ea36 into independently shippable sub-beads, each
small enough to land in one execute-bead pass with focused tests:

  ddx-<new>-D1: workerDispatchAdapter + Drain button — empty-spec defaults to
                Profile:"default"; no other synthesis. Unit test +
                dashboard wiring. Closes AC #2 partially.

  ddx-<new>-D2a: Default path stops calling ResolveTierModelRef /
                 ResolveProfileLadder. Add --escalate flag that re-enables
                 them. Tests assert call graph. Closes AC #2 fully.

  ddx-<new>-D3: Preflight gate before Claim using upstream's typed
                ErrHarnessModelIncompatible. Targeted test using upstream
                test seam (file the upstream test-seam ask as a separate
                ddx-agent bead if missing). Closes AC #3.

  ddx-<new>-D4: Config breaking-change migration. Hard-error on
                default_harness; warn-and-ignore on profile_ladders/
                model_overrides; schema update; migration fixture tests;
                CHANGELOG migration guide. Closes AC #4.

  ddx-<new>-D5: CLI flag-surface parity tests for ddx agent run / work /
                execute-bead with the four override permutations from AC #5.
                Closes AC #5.

  ddx-<new>-Audit: Audit pass for AC #7 (compensating logic removal +
                   dead-code orphan detector in CI). Closes AC #7.

  ddx-<new>-Regression: AC #10 regression-guard integration test that
                        reproduces the 19-burn drain bug; AC #2's
                        integration sibling. Closes AC #10 (and validates
                        D1+D2+D3 together).

  ddx-<new>-Playwright: AC #6 frontend golden against the rewired Drain
                        path. Last in sequence — depends on D1+D2+D3 +
                        Regression. Closes AC #6.

  ddx-<new>-Docs: Cross-link DDx CLAUDE.md / AGENTS.md to upstream profile
                  catalog + override precedence + best-provider contract
                  docs (AC #9 second half — CHANGELOG half is already in
                  CHANGELOG.md from the prior commit).

  ddx-<new>-D6-followup: Decision-trace consumers in route-status /
                         providers UI — explicitly out of scope per the
                         bead description but should be filed now so the
                         tracking exists (bead description's own AC #8
                         requires this).

ddx-fdd3ea36 should remain open as the umbrella tracker until all
sub-beads close, at which point AC #2..#10 can be re-verified end-to-end
in one final review pass.

NO CODE CHANGES IN THIS ATTEMPT.

rationale: Bead: ddx-fdd3ea36 — Adopt ddx-agent routing point release (DDx-side consumer epic)

Disposition: no_changes — this is an epic/tracker bead whose work has been
deliberately decomposed into nine child beads. Some are closed; four remain
open. The tracker cannot close until those four close. There is no
implementation work that belongs at the epic level itself.

================================================================================
What is done
================================================================================

Upstream prerequisite (AC #1):
  All six routing-plan beads in ~/Projects/agent/.ddx/ are closed and merged
  into tagged ddx-agent releases (v0.9.10..v0.9.24):
    agent-191a74f9  profiles (default/local/standard)         closed
    agent-bab52778  supported-models allow-list + gating      closed
    agent-dfabb10b  ErrHarnessModelIncompatible / PinConflict closed
    agent-53f38d95  public RouteDecision.Candidates           closed
    agent-0dafc7f0  CostUSDPer1kTokens + cost tiebreak        closed
    agent-1f46cf22  routing documentation                     closed
  cli/go.mod pins github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/agent v0.9.23, which contains
  every routing-plan commit (verified via `git tag --contains <sha>` against
  closing_commit_sha for each upstream bead).

DDx-side child beads already closed:
  ddx-967e9802  routing-bump (go.mod pin + CHANGELOG)         closed
  ddx-755f5881  routing-default-path (D1 + D2)                closed   → AC #2
  ddx-98e6e9ef  routing-preflight-gate (D3)                   closed   → AC #3
  ddx-dd4c423d  routing-drain-regression test                 closed   → AC #10

Code evidence the closed work is in tree:
  cli/internal/agent/profile_ladder.go:20-21
    test seams verify ResolveProfileLadder/ResolveTierModelRef are NOT called
    on the default path (per ddx-755f5881 AC #3)
  cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go:84-120
    DispatchWorker normalizes empty input to {Profile: "default"} only
  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:360-392
    routing preflight gate runs BEFORE Claim at line 394 (D3, ddx-98e6e9ef)
  cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1683-1684
    --escalate / --override-model flags exist; tier ladder + model_overrides
    are off by default (consistent with D2/D4 design)
  cli/internal/config/types.go:230-234
    migration note: agent.routing.default_harness was REMOVED in ddx-87fb72c2

================================================================================
What is open (blocks tracker close)
================================================================================

ddx-87fb72c2  routing-config-deprecation                      open  → AC #4
              hard error on default_harness; ladders/overrides opt-in only;
              CHANGELOG migration entry. (Note: the field-removal half appears
              landed per types.go:230-234 — verify what residual scope this
              bead still owns before executing it.)

ddx-2589e256  routing-overrides-e2e                           open  → AC #5
              --harness/--model/--profile flag plumbing parity tests for
              ResolveRoute(Profile, Harness, Model) shape; cost-tiebreak e2e;
              typed-error surfacing for incompatible combos.

ddx-950321ef  routing-frontend-goldens                        open  → AC #6
              Playwright drain-queue fixtures matching the real failing
              config; Drain button → live-endpoint-backed harness;
              never gemini+non-gemini-model; never 19-burn.

ddx-653f6ac9  routing-cleanup                                 open  → AC #7
              dead-code detector for orphaned compensating logic;
              remove residual allow-list / exact-pin / cost-scoring code
              that re-implements upstream behavior.

ddx-a0cf398b  routing-followups                               open  → AC #8
              file the route-status / providers-UI decision-trace consumer
              bead; close out D6/D7 housekeeping.

================================================================================
Why this bead does not get implementation in this pass
================================================================================

1. The epic's AC is the union of its children's AC. Every remaining AC item
   (#4, #5, #6, #7, #8, plus the documentation half of #9) is the contract of
   a specific open child bead. Implementing them here would either duplicate
   work that the child bead will redo, or steal scope that another worker
   may already be planning to claim.

2. The bead description's "Sequencing" section explicitly orders:
     1. upstream lands (done)
     2. go.mod bump (done — ddx-967e9802)
     3. D1+D2+D3+D5 land together (D1+D2=ddx-755f5881 done; D3=ddx-98e6e9ef
        done; D5=ddx-2589e256 OPEN)
     4. D4 (ddx-87fb72c2) lands with or right after the above (OPEN)
     5. follow-ups (ddx-a0cf398b, ddx-950321ef, ddx-653f6ac9) (OPEN)
   The tracker is meant to close once steps 3–5 complete via their child
   beads, not to carry independent code.

3. Attempting any of {D4, override e2e, playwright goldens, dead-code sweep}
   in this single execute-bead pass would land a sprawling cross-cutting
   commit that violates the per-child-bead acceptance contracts and is
   hard to review. Per CLAUDE.md "DOWITYTD" and the merge-policy memory,
   the right action is to execute the children individually.

================================================================================
What a follow-up attempt should do
================================================================================

This tracker should NOT be re-queued for direct execution. Instead:

  a) Execute the four open child beads in their natural order:
        ddx-87fb72c2 (D4 — config deprecation residue + CHANGELOG)
        ddx-2589e256 (D5 — override flag e2e)
        ddx-950321ef (D6-equivalent — Playwright goldens)
        ddx-653f6ac9 (cleanup / dead-code sweep)
        ddx-a0cf398b (file follow-up beads)
     Each closes one or two AC items on this tracker.

  b) When all five close, re-open this tracker briefly to:
        - verify every AC item maps to a closed child or in-tree evidence
        - update DDx CHANGELOG (AC #9) if not already done by ddx-967e9802
        - close ddx-fdd3ea36 with a closing-comment that points at each
          child bead's closing commit.

That close step is documentation/bookkeeping — a single small commit, well
suited to a future execute-bead pass once the children are green.

No code changes were made in this pass. Workspace is clean.

## Scope
- ddx bead review <bead-id> [--iter N] [--output <file>]
- Pulls bead AC from the bead record
- Pulls commit diff via git show (latest successful execute-bead result_rev by default, or --from-rev)
- Reads governing-doc slugs from bead 'spec-id' / 'refs' fields and excerpts the relevant sections
- Renders a prompt template asking the agent to grade each AC item and produce structured APPROVE / REQUEST_CHANGES / BLOCK
- Output is the prompt body (stdout or to a file); pipe into ddx agent run --prompt @-

## Context
Observed during axon ADR-018 loop: I wrote /tmp/review-A1.md by hand twice (iter 1 and iter 2). Both prompts had the same structure. This bead (ddx-fe63969f) duplicates work already committed under bead
ddx-89bc2db4 in commit ef7efd46 ("feat(federation): hub-side fan-out
client/merger with timeout, max-concurrency, partial results").

Acceptance-criteria mapping to existing code:

AC1 — Fan-out client with timeout + concurrency bounds.
  FanOutClient at cli/internal/federation/fanout.go:125-141 with
  MaxConcurrency (default 8) + PerNodeTimeout (default 5s). Concurrency
  is enforced by a buffered channel semaphore at fanout.go:226. Per-node
  timeout is applied via context.WithTimeout in callOne at fanout.go:329.

AC2 — Merger marks partials; never silently drops.
  FanOutResult at fanout.go:103-122 splits results into Responses,
  Errors, Skipped, StatusUpdates, and Nodes. Every input spoke produces
  exactly one NodeResult (fanout.go:225, 266-289). NodeOutcomeKind
  values OutcomeTimeout / OutcomeOffline / OutcomeHTTPError /
  OutcomeSkipped (fanout.go:46-65) distinguish partial-failure modes.
  Per-node timeouts deliberately do NOT flip status to offline
  (fanout.go:353-361), preserving the registry's stale signal.

AC3 — Per-spoke capability check skips incompatible spokes gracefully.
  preflightSkip at fanout.go:295-323 filters by RequiredCapabilities
  against SpokeRecord.Capabilities (ignoring the synthetic @identity:
  fingerprint cap), emitting OutcomeSkipped + SkipReasonMissingCapability.
  Version incompatibility is also handled (SkipReasonIncompatibleVersion
  via the Handshake gate).

AC4 — Tests cover timeout, partial, and capability skip.
  cli/internal/federation/fanout_test.go covers:
    - TestFanOut_PerNodeTimeoutDoesNotBlockOthers (timeout)
    - TestFanOut_PartialResultOnOneFailure (partial)
    - TestFanOut_StaleVsOfflineDistinct (partial / status-update policy)
    - TestFanOut_MidResponseDisconnect (partial / transport failure)
    - TestFanOut_HTTPErrorIsNotOffline (partial / classification)
    - TestFanOut_MissingCapabilitySkipped (capability skip)
    - TestFanOut_VersionIncompatibleSpokesSkipped (version skip)
    - TestFanOut_MaxConcurrencyEnforced (concurrency bound)
    - TestFanOut_AllSpokesSucceed, TestFanOut_PostsGraphQLPayload,
      TestFanOut_RejectsEmptyRequest (happy path / contract).

The verification command above re-runs the suite and exits 0 when all
AC-covering tests pass.

.ddx/beads.jsonl:1244:{"acceptance":"(1) Root cause documented in code comment next to SynthesizeCommit. (2) SynthesizeCommit completes with exit 0 when only change is in a gitignored directory. (3) Regression test: a project with non-empty .ddx/agent-logs and no real changes yields SynthesizeCommit → (false, nil) (no commit, no error). (4) Regression test: a project with one real modification + non-empty .ddx/agent-logs yields (true, nil) with only the real change committed. (5) ddx work --once drains ddx-672cf2bf or another ready bead end-to-end without hitting staging-changes exit 1.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","created_at":"2026-04-20T14:57:38.058551192Z","description":"Blocks `ddx work` whenever the gitignored runtime directories `.ddx/agent-logs/` and `.ddx/workers/` have any content. Every real-world project hits this after the first agent run.

## Symptom

Every tier attempt in the execute-loop aborts:

```
pre-execute-bead checkpoint: staging changes: exit status 1
```

## Root cause

cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:363 `RealGitOps.SynthesizeCommit` runs:

```
git -C <dir> add -A -- . ':(exclude).ddx/agent-logs' ':(exclude).ddx/workers' ':(exclude).ddx/executions/*/embedded' ':(exclude).ddx/executions/*/no_changes_rationale.txt' ':(exclude).claude/skills' ':(exclude).agents/skills'
```

When `.ddx/agent-logs/` and `.ddx/workers/` exist as untracked directories (normal after the first agent run), and both are in .gitignore (lines 421-422), git add emits:

```
The following paths are ignored by one of your .gitignore files:
.ddx/agent-logs
.ddx/workers
hint: Use -f if you really want to add them.
```

AND exits with status 1. The `:(exclude)` magic pathspec is not sufficient to silence this exit-1 in git's add behavior with `-C` form — even `-c advice.addIgnoredFile=false` does not suppress it.

## Fix candidates (not pre-decided)

1. Check `git status --porcelain` first; only run `git add` if there are tracked changes to stage.
2. Enumerate dirty files in Go (via `git status --porcelain`), filter out excluded paths, pass the survivors explicitly to `git add --`.
3. Distinguish stderr "benign ignored-files warning" from real errors; accept exit 1 when that's the only message.
4. Move `.ddx/agent-logs/` and `.ddx/workers/` out of the repo tree so they're outside the pathspec entirely.

Prefer (1) or (2) — they're honest about the intent "stage only real modifications." Option (3) is fragile against git message changes; (4) touches a lot of downstream tooling.

## Evidence

Worker: worker-20260420T145025-61dc. Bead ddx-672cf2bf. All three tier attempts (cheap/standard/smart) failed identically. Confirmed reproducible via manual run of the exact add command in `/Users/erik/Projects/ddx`.

Pattern:
- Use cli/internal/agent/integration_helper_test.go newScriptHarnessRepo(t, beadCount) to build an isolated repo with real git.
- Use runGitInteg (from cli/internal/git/git_basic_test.go) for any supplemental git invocations, so GIT_* env is scrubbed.
- Replace fakeExecuteBeadGit with the RealLandingGitOps flow used in cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land_test.go.
- Replace fakeAgentRunner with the script harness (cli/internal/agent/script.go) driven by a per-attempt directive file. When the test needs to simulate 'agent emits no commits' or 'agent emits a dirty worktree', encode that as script directives rather than a canned Result struct.
- Preserve every assertion — the point is that the test now exercises real git + real merge/preserve semantics, not that the surface shrinks.

Scope limit: ONLY this test. Do not migrate any other test in the same file; touching another test expands blast radius outside the bead.

Verification: cd cli && go test ./cmd -run '^TestExecuteBeadDirtyWorktreeWithoutCommits$' -count=1 passes. No struct ending in -GitOps or -Runner that fakes the thing under test is referenced from this test after the migration.

Governing concern: concerns.md §testing 'no mocks, period' + 'never mock the thing you are testing'. 2. Clicking a graph node navigates to the document/artifact detail without a full page reload.
3. Pressing Back from a document opened via graph click returns to graph with the same zoom/pan position.
4. Filter chips filter visible nodes + edges; node colors reflect staleness (green/amber/red).
5. 'View document list' link on graph page navigates to /artifacts?mediaType=text%2Fmarkdown.
6. graph.spec.ts covers: initial render → pan/zoom → click node → verify navigation → Back → verify viewport restored → apply staleness filter → verify node count changes.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","claimed-at":"2026-05-01T03:06:28Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"3396374","closing_commit_sha":"7123e6ebd786c63225500b5544b95be2901b60d4","created_at":"2026-04-30T15:10:47.923008659Z","description":"Fix the document dependency graph interactivity. Addresses US-081 AC which is specced but unimplemented.

Bugs to fix:
1. Pan/zoom sizing race: D3Graph.svelte uses el.clientWidth at init time which may be 0. Replace with ResizeObserver: set up simulation once observer fires with non-zero dimensions. This also handles window resize.
2. Node click = no handler: nodeGroup has mouseenter/mousemove/mouseleave but no onclick. Add click handler that navigates to /nodes/:nodeId/projects/:projectId/documents/:path (for markdown artifacts) or /artifacts/:artifactId (for other types). Distinguish by mediaType from graph node data.

Viewport persistence:
- Save zoom transform (x, y, k) to component state or URL param (e.g. ?zoom=1.2&pan=120,45) on each zoom/pan end event.
- On Back navigation, restore the saved transform before the D3 effect runs.

New features:
- Filter chips on graph page: filter nodes by mediaType (markdown, image, unknown) and staleness (fresh, stale, missing). Hidden nodes removed from simulation; edges to hidden nodes removed.
- Node color = staleness: green=fresh, amber=stale, red=missing (not dependsOn.length as today).
- 'View document list' link on graph page → /documents (which redirects to /artifacts).
- 'View in Graph' link on artifact detail page (once ddx-d7d1119d exists) → /graph with node pre-selected.

Review area: host+user ddx-server planning stack after the server model evolved beyond the earlier multi-project topology draft. Evidence covers FEAT-002, FEAT-008, FEAT-013, FEAT-014, SD-019, TP-002, the fresh-eyes server review, and the updated requirements for a per-user host daemon, localhost HTTP UI, SQLite-backed runtime/index state, embedded-agent progress visibility, provider dashboarding, replay-backed fixtures, and Playwright coverage for every new UI surface.
</context-digest>
Update the governing server planning docs so they describe the current intended host+user ddx-server model before follow-on server design and implementation beads continue.

## Goals
- Reconcile FEAT-002, FEAT-008, FEAT-013, FEAT-014, SD-019, and TP-002 around a single host+user ddx-server instance with explicit project registry, worker management boundaries, and queue semantics
- Define the split between git-backed project truth, tracked execution evidence, and host-local runtime/index state backed by an embedded database
- Define the current transport/security model: localhost unauthenticated HTTP for UI/API now, explicit future transport/auth evolution later
- Add the embedded-agent progress, provider dashboard, replay-backed fixture, and Playwright coverage requirements to the governed server plan
- Make the scheduling boundary explicit: server may allocate workers across projects, but bead ordering remains owned by each project queue

## Boundaries
- Keep Axon and multi-host service-backed topology as later-stage work
- Do not reopen the already-shipped single-project execute-bead loop contract except where the new server plan must reference it","events":[{"actor":"erik","body":"checkpointing dirty worktree: git stash: error: could not write index

.ddx/beads.jsonl:1273:{"acceptance":"API-001 and FEAT-006 agree on a single owner for required post-run checks and land/preserve semantics; no implementation path requires running those steps twice around one execute-bead attempt","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-10T12:07:31Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"2428934","closing_commit_sha":"3b26aac39659d65ea161498720609c15b2607726","created_at":"2026-04-10T05:54:17.994514899Z","description":"Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: docs/helix/02-design/contracts/API-001-execute-bead-supervisor-contract.md:65
Category: correctness
Severity: high
Description: API-001 says the supervisor should run `ddx agent execute-bead` and then separately run required post-run checks, classify the outcome, and apply land/preserve semantics. FEAT-006 already defines `ddx agent execute-bead` itself as the canonical workflow that resolves execution documents, runs required executions, evaluates merge eligibility, and either lands or preserves the iteration. Implementing API-001 as written would either duplicate required executions and landing logic around `execute-bead` or force FEAT-006 to become stale without saying so, which makes the command/supervisor boundary ambiguous for the first shipped worker.
Suggested fix: Make one layer authoritative. Pass 1 correctness: API-001 duplicates post-run and land/preserve ownership around Unified interface for dispatching work to AI coding agents.

Supports multiple harnesses (codex, claude, gemini, etc.) with output capture,
token tracking, session logging, and multi-agent quorum.

Examples:
  ddx agent run --harness codex --prompt task.md
  ddx agent run --quorum majority --harnesses codex,claude --prompt task.md
  ddx agent list
  ddx agent capabilities codex
  ddx agent doctor
  ddx agent log

Usage:
  ddx agent [flags]
  ddx agent [command]

Available Commands:
  benchmark    Run a benchmark suite comparing agent harnesses
  capabilities Show agent model and reasoning-level capabilities
  condense     Filter raw agent output to progress-relevant lines
  doctor       Check agent harness health
  list         List available agent harnesses
  log          Show agent session history
  replay       Replay a bead with a different agent harness or model
  run          Invoke an agent with a prompt
  usage        Show per-harness token and cost summary

Flags:
  -h, --help   help for agent

Global Flags:
      --config string              config file (default is $HOME/.ddx.yml)
      --library-base-path string   override path for DDx library location
  -v, --verbose                    verbose output

Use "ddx agent [command] --help" for more information about a command., and it validates candidate readiness before resolving the governing base snapshot that FEAT-006 says must control execution-document evaluation.
Pass 2 integration: API-001 uses the generic validator to choose the best candidate even though HELIX owns eligibility policy and SD-004 already defines deterministic ready ordering, leaving queue selection undefined.
Pass 3 concern-aware quality: skipped because the reviewed change is documentation-only design work with no new runtime attack surface or toolchain drift.
Pass 4 operational learnings: no AGENTS.md change required; no learnings bead required.

Description: API-001 says the supervisor should read the ready bead set and choose the best candidate using the generic execution-ready validator. But the same contract says HELIX owns which beads are eligible for the loop, and SD-004 already defines deterministic ready ordering as priority then stable snapshot order. The generic DDx validator can say whether one bead is structurally executable; it does not define queue priority or workflow policy. Leaving candidate choice as 'best' via the validator either invents hidden DDx scheduling policy or makes the first worker's queue behavior undefined.

.ddx/beads.jsonl:1548:{"acceptance":"execute-bead rebases the worktree result onto current HEAD before attempting ff-merge; when the rebase succeeds the result lands without a merge commit; when the rebase conflicts the iteration is preserved under the hidden ref and the target branch remains unchanged; and tests cover the case where the target branch advanced during execution","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-10T02:28:56Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"4071725","closing_commit_sha":"65134ca7caedd43b6dab6e567d001b36fb32b665","created_at":"2026-04-09T13:00:00Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"hx-exec-bead-stash-fix","depends_on_id":"hx-exec-bead-stash-fix","type":"blocks"}],"description":"FEAT-006 step 10 specifies 'land by rebase + fast-forward semantics' but the implementation only attempts `git merge --ff-only`.

## Current behavior
If the target branch has advanced since the base revision, the ff-merge fails and the result is preserved under a hidden ref instead of being rebased and landed.

## Fix required
Before attempting ff-merge, rebase the worktree commits onto the current branch HEAD. If the rebase succeeds, then ff-merge and keep linear history. If the rebase fails because of conflicts, preserve the iteration under the hidden ref, leave the target branch untouched, and report the non-landed result deterministically.","id":"hx-exec-bead-rebase-ff","issue_type":"bug","labels":["helix","area:agent"],"owner":"erik","parent":"hx-epic-ddx-agent-migration","priority":0,"status":"closed","title":"Implement rebase + ff semantics for execute-bead land flow","updated_at":"2026-04-10T02:31:23.760412906Z"}
.ddx/beads.jsonl:1549:{"acceptance":"After execute-bead completes, dirty worktree changes are either restored via git stash pop or the user is explicitly warned with the stash ref so they can restore manually","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-09T19:15:50Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"1385203","closing_commit_sha":"ada469a58561d9795471b0b02d10aa5c8a7f94aa","created_at":"2026-04-09T13:00:00Z","description":"execute-bead stashes dirty worktree changes but never restores them after execution.

## Current behavior
When execute-bead detects a dirty worktree, it calls git stash to checkpoint changes but never calls git stash pop. User's in-progress work remains silently stashed.

## Fix required
Add git stash pop (or git stash apply) in the defer cleanup after the worktree is removed, or document that stash is intentional and warn the user with the stash ref.","id":"hx-exec-bead-stash-fix","issue_type":"bug","labels":["helix","area:agent","severity:high"],"owner":"erik","parent":"hx-epic-ddx-agent-migration","priority":0,"status":"closed","title":"Fix execute-bead dirty worktree stash/restore bug","updated_at":"2026-04-09T19:20:21.950105538Z"}

File: docs/helix/02-design/contracts/API-001-execute-bead-supervisor-contract.md:67
Category: correctness
Severity: high
Description: The updated contract still says the supervisor claims the bead, creates an isolated execution worktree, runs `ddx agent execute-bead` inside that worktree, and then removes the worktree. FEAT-006 defines `ddx agent execute-bead` itself as the workflow that creates the isolated worktree (step 4) and always removes it after preserve/land handling (step 12), and the shipped command implementation already does exactly that in `cli/cmd/agent_execute_bead.go:261-274`. As written, the first shipped supervisor cannot follow API-001 without layering a second worktree lifecycle around one execute-bead attempt or introducing an undocumented lower-level mode. That leaves the core ownership boundary ambiguous and makes the contract non-executable for the intended wrapper design.
Suggested fix: Make one layer own the worktree lifecycle. Either change API-001 so the supervisor invokes `ddx agent execute-bead` from the project root and only reacts to its result, or introduce and document a lower-level execute-bead primitive that accepts an already-created worktree and update FEAT-006/API-001 together.","id":"hx-f9928f75","issue_type":"task","labels":["helix","phase:build","review-finding","severity:high","area:api","area:agent","area:docs"],"owner":"erik","priority":0,"spec-id":"docs/helix/02-design/contracts/API-001-execute-bead-supervisor-contract.md","status":"closed","title":"correctness: avoid duplicating execute-bead worktree ownership in API-001","updated_at":"2026-04-10T12:45:39.684926309Z"}

data.text: I found an existing report file in the target docs area, so I’m checking whether the bead is asking for a revision versus a new artifact. I’m also pulling the governing policy text and the current report side by side so I can map the acceptance criteria to specific sections before editing.

data.output:
# Recent Failed Attempt Causes And Readiness Checkability

Evidence window: `2026-05-05T01:31:09Z` through `2026-05-06T15:21:21Z`.
Sources sampled: `.ddx/beads.jsonl` event streams plus
`.ddx/executions/<attempt-id>/result.json` and
`.ddx/executions/<attempt-id>/manifest.json` for the attempts below.

This report groups the observed failures into the five buckets required by the
bead acceptance. The point is not perfect root-cause proof; it is a durable,
operator-facing estimate of what kind of failure happened and whether
bead-lifecycle readiness can catch it before claim.

## Bucket Summary

| Bucket | Example bead / attempt | Observed snippet | Can readiness check before claim? | Owning follow-up surface |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Bead readiness | `ddx-9c5bca8f` / `20260506T092126-fa2440e8` | `The Axon backend boundary ... are already present in the current worktree` | Yes. This is the class bead readiness is supposed to catch early. | `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go` pre-claim intake / readiness lint; `docs/helix/06-iterate/reliability-principles.md` and ADR-023 policy. |
| System readiness | `ddx-4ab66676` / `20260506T154152-4a9c452c`; `ddx-d0c656b0` / `20260506T160123-7b468ece` | `No space left on device`, `.git/index.lock` exists, `review-error: transport`, `claude quota-exhausted` | Mixed. Local disk and stale git-lock conditions are checkable before claim; provider quota exhaustion is only heuristically visible. | Host-resource preflight and git-lock checks in `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go` plus the review transport path in the execute loop. |
| Post-attempt | `ddx-9f20a4bd` / `20260506T103413-b9f566cd` | `TestGraphQLPerfMatrix_Baseline ... p95=57.28ms > 50ms budget` | No. This only shows up after the targeted build/test run or checkpoint. | Post-attempt triage in `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go` and the test budget surface in `cli/internal/server/perf`. |
| Close-policy | `ddx-8d747049` / `20260505T160236-8db426ba` | `(rationale absent)` and `agent exited without a commit or no_changes_rationale.txt` | No. This is a closure-contract failure after the attempt, not a claim-time readiness issue. | `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go` close path, the no_changes contract, and tracker-close policy. |
| Unknown | `ddx-ff7c8ec9` / `20260505T012605-cd23d1fd` | `review-error: unparseable JSON verdict: no JSON object found` | No. The error is real, but the root cause is not recoverable from the payload alone. | Reviewer contract / parser boundary in the review pipeline. |

## Bucket Notes

### Bead Readiness

Representative failure: `ddx-9c5bca8f` at `20260506T092126-fa2440e8`.
The attempt returned `task_no_changes` because the targeted Axon backend and
its tests were already present in-tree. That is a true bead-readiness signal:
the work was already done, so the loop should be able to recognize that before
claiming the bead.

Readiness verdict: yes, this bucket is the one bead-lifecycle readiness should
own before claim.

### System Readiness

Representative failures:

- `ddx-4ab66676` at `20260506T154152-4a9c452c` hit `No space left on device`
  while creating the isolated worktree and execution artifacts.
- `ddx-4ab66676` in the same attempt also surfaced `.git/index.lock` contention
  in the pre-execute checkpoint.
- `ddx-d0c656b0` at `20260506T160123-7b468ece` later failed review with
  `review-error: transport` and `claude quota-exhausted`.

Readiness verdict: mixed. Disk-space and stale-lock problems are pre-claim
checks. Provider quota exhaustion is only partially checkable because it
depends on external provider state, not just local repository state.

### Post-Attempt

Representative failure: `ddx-9f20a4bd` at `20260506T103413-b9f566cd`.
The attempt reached verification, then failed because the package-wide command
hit `TestGraphQLPerfMatrix_Baseline` above budget. That is a post-attempt
problem: the code path is only visible after the test or checkpoint runs.

Readiness verdict: no, not before claim. Bead-lifecycle can only detect this
after a targeted verification pass.

### Close-Policy

Representative failure: `ddx-8d747049` at `20260505T160236-8db426ba`.
The loop had work to do, but the attempt ended with `agent exited without a
commit or no_changes_rationale.txt`. That is not a code defect; it is a
closure-contract failure.

Readiness verdict: no. The only reliable detector is the post-attempt close
path, because the issue is about missing final evidence, not claim-time state.

### Unknown

Representative failure: `ddx-ff7c8ec9` at `20260505T012605-cd23d1fd`.
The reviewer emitted `review-error: unparseable JSON verdict: no JSON object
found`. The payload proves something went wrong, but not enough to classify it
as provider, host, bead readiness, or close-policy.

Readiness verdict: no. Unknowns remain a post-hoc investigation bucket until
the review contract or parser boundary records a more specific cause.

## Closing Notes

Documentation-only waiver: this report adds analysis only. No new Go test is
required because it does not add generated references or executable code.

The next improvement surface is the bead-lifecycle readiness and triage
taxonomy: local host checks, provider/reviewer transport classification, and

instead of being left to ad hoc event inspection.

# ADR-023: Bead Lifecycle Quality Policy

**Status:** Proposed (Accepted after operator review)
**Date:** 2026-05-04
**Authors:** bead `ddx-9210f95a`

## Context

Reliability principle P7 ("BEAD = PROMPT") is planned in
`docs/helix/06-iterate/reliability-principles.md` by bead `ddx-06b77652`.
The principle says a bead's description and acceptance criteria must be enough
for a competent sub-agent to execute without hand-curation, but it does not
define the enforcement mechanism, waiver model, staged rollout, or recovery UX.

The quality gap is already visible in evidence:

- Audit 1, `.ddx/executions/20260503T195715-4725673a/bead-quality-audit-2026-05-03.md`,
  found repeated misses in root-cause detail, concrete test names, and
  self-contained scope.
- Audit 1 snapshot,
  `.ddx/executions/20260503T155638-bead57f0cb9e/bead-quality-audit-2026-05-03.md`,
  directly operationalizes P7 and records the first 20-bead scoring pass.
- Audit 2,
  `.ddx/executions/20260504T030000-audit2/bead-quality-audit-remaining-2026-05-04.md`,
  extended the scan across the remaining open queue and calls out evidence bead
  `ddx-f339c399`.
- `docs/helix/06-iterate/bead-authoring-template.md` is the canonical
  8-criterion authoring rubric and explicitly defines valid criterion skips for
  epic, doc-only, deletion, and rename beads.

Three existing feature specs already own the affected surfaces:

- FEAT-004 owns bead schema, validation hooks, labels, and evidence.
- FEAT-010 owns `ddx try`, `ddx work`, run records, and attempt outcomes.
- FEAT-011 owns the DDx skill packaging model that gives agents reusable
  workflow guidance.

## Decision

DDx adopts bead-lifecycle quality enforcement as policy over the existing
bead, execution, and skill surfaces. No new bead schema fields are introduced.
No new top-level FEAT is created.

### Policy

Every automated bead attempt has two quality checkpoints:

1. **Pre-claim intake.** Before `ddx work` claims a bead or `ddx try` launches
   the implementation invocation, DDx evaluates actionability, scope, and
   decomposition risk. It checks the bead description, acceptance criteria,
   labels, parent, dependency metadata, spec-id, prior attempt history, and the
   canonical rubric in `docs/helix/06-iterate/bead-authoring-template.md`.
2. **Post-attempt triage.** After an attempt finalizes, DDx triages the result
   against the same lifecycle quality policy so a low-quality prompt failure,
   missing rationale, empty review block, or structurally ambiguous outcome is
   classified and surfaced consistently.

Both checkpoints invoke the same nested bead-lifecycle workflow skill under the
`ddx` skill tree. The skill is responsible for translating the rubric into
agent-readable findings; DDx remains responsible for hook timing, evidence
placement, and outcome classification.

Post-attempt triage classifications feed
[`TD-031`](../technical-designs/TD-031-bead-state-machine.md). ADR-023 does not
define final queue mutation policy; TD-031 remains the source of truth for
whether an attempt closes, stays open for human triage, becomes blocked, is
superseded, or receives a retry cooldown.

### Staged Rollout And Factory Mode

The default rollout is WARN-ONLY.

- WARN-ONLY mode reports the lint score, missing criteria, waiver matches, and
  suggested remediation, then proceeds with dispatch.
- BLOCK mode is opt-in until the queue baseline confirms that ordinary
  execution-ready beads can consistently satisfy the rubric.
- BLOCK mode may stop only on a valid low lint score after applicable rubric
  skips and label waivers are applied. It must not stop on hook crashes,
  missing skill files, transient filesystem errors, or malformed lint output.
- Reliable factory mode is BLOCK mode plus default adversarial review. In that
  mode, poorly specified work is improved, decomposed, or blocked before claim
  rather than allowed to consume implementation attempts.

This makes the policy measurable before it becomes a dispatch gate and avoids
freezing the queue while legacy beads are being retrofitted.

### Waiver Model

Waivers exist at two levels:

- **Rubric-level by bead type.** The skips documented in
  `docs/helix/06-iterate/bead-authoring-template.md` are built into lint:
  doc-only beads may omit criterion (c) and (d), epic beads may satisfy those
  criteria through children, and pure deletion or rename beads may omit
  criterion (d) while preserving behavior.
- **Per-bead labels.** Rare exceptions use labels of the form
  `lint-waiver:<criterion>`, for example `lint-waiver:c`. Labels are the
  durable waiver store because FEAT-004 already defines labels as free-form
  bead metadata and no schema change is warranted.

Operator override uses `--force --reason <text>`. The override records an event
in the bead evidence stream naming the criterion, mode, actor, reason, and lint
summary. It does not silently bypass the hook and it does not mutate the rubric.

### Fail-Open Behavior

The lifecycle hooks follow reliability principle P1's fail-open posture for
infrastructure failures.

- If the hook cannot run, the workflow skill is missing, output cannot be
  parsed, or evidence cannot be written, DDx records the infrastructure failure
  and proceeds.
- In WARN-ONLY mode, all valid lint results proceed after reporting.
- In BLOCK mode, only a valid post-waiver lint result below the configured
  threshold blocks dispatch.
- Post-attempt triage failures never erase the attempt result. They annotate the
  report and evidence so retry policy and operators can distinguish "agent made
  a bad attempt" from "quality infrastructure failed."

### Safe Improvement, Decomposition, And Recovery UX

Pre-claim intake may update a bead before execution only when the update is
intent-preserving and grounded in durable context. Safe improvements include
normalizing the description into the authoring template, adding discovered
file:line evidence, adding an obvious subsystem test command, and wiring
deterministic labels, parent, or dependencies. Intake must block for human input
instead of inventing acceptance criteria, changing scope, choosing between
conflicting requirements, or guessing a missing governing artifact.

If intake finds the bead too broad, it decomposes before claim. Every parent AC
must map to at least one child AC or be explicitly marked `needs_human` or
`non_scope`; token-overlap metrics are heuristics, not proof of preservation.
The parent is blocked/decomposed with child ids and the AC map in evidence.

When BLOCK mode stops dispatch, the operator-facing output must be actionable.
It prints:

- bead id, score, mode, and blocked criteria
- the missing fields or malformed sections in plain language
- the waiver labels already applied and the labels that would waive each
  failed criterion
- suggested `ddx bead update` commands for adding description, acceptance,
  labels, parent, notes, or dependency metadata
- the `--force --reason` form for exceptional dispatch, with the note that an
  evidence event will be recorded

Recovery output must not require reading the lint implementation or an
out-of-band report to fix an ordinary authoring problem.

## Consequences

- FEAT-004 owns the label-based waiver storage and the no-new-schema rule.
- FEAT-010 owns hook insertion in `ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime`, attempt
  classification, and the `OutcomeReason` report field.
- FEAT-011 owns nested workflow-skill packaging so the same rubric guidance can
  be reused by lint, triage, review, breakdown, replay, and benchmark flows.
- Legacy beads can continue running in WARN-ONLY while the queue is baselined.
- Once BLOCK mode is enabled, dispatch quality becomes explicit and auditable
  instead of depending on operator memory.

## Non-Goals

- A new FEAT for bead quality; the existing feature specs own the affected
  surfaces.
- The separate `in_progress` eligibility bug.
- Cross-project skill packaging, which remains deferred to FEAT-015.
- New fields in `.ddx/beads.jsonl`; waivers use labels and evidence events.

## References

- `ddx-06b77652` — RELIABILITY-PRINCIPLES bead for P7.
- `ddx-f339c399` — evidence bead called out by Audit 2.
- `docs/helix/06-iterate/bead-authoring-template.md` — canonical rubric and
  criterion-skip policy.
- `.ddx/executions/20260503T195715-4725673a/bead-quality-audit-2026-05-03.md`
  — Audit 1.
- `.ddx/executions/20260503T155638-bead57f0cb9e/bead-quality-audit-2026-05-03.md`
  — Audit 1 scoring snapshot.
- `.ddx/executions/20260504T030000-audit2/bead-quality-audit-remaining-2026-05-04.md`
  — Audit 2.
- `docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-004-beads.md` — bead metadata,
  validation, labels, and evidence.
- `docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md` — try/work lifecycle
  and attempt outcomes.
- `docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-011-skills.md` — DDx skill packaging.

# Recent Failed Attempt Causes And Readiness Checkability

Evidence window: `2026-05-05T01:31:09Z` through `2026-05-06T15:21:21Z`.
Sources sampled: `.ddx/beads.jsonl` event streams plus
`.ddx/executions/<attempt-id>/result.json` and
`.ddx/executions/<attempt-id>/manifest.json` for the attempts below.

This report groups the observed failures into the five buckets required by the
bead acceptance. The point is not perfect root-cause proof; it is a durable,
operator-facing estimate of what kind of failure happened and whether
bead-lifecycle readiness can catch it before claim.

## Bucket Summary

## Bucket Notes

### Bead Readiness

Representative failure: `ddx-9c5bca8f` at `20260506T092126-fa2440e8`.
The attempt returned `task_no_changes` because the targeted Axon backend and
its tests were already present in-tree. That is a true bead-readiness signal:
the work was already done, so the loop should be able to recognize that before
claiming the bead.

Readiness verdict: yes, this bucket is the one bead-lifecycle readiness should
own before claim.

### System Readiness

Representative failures:

  while creating the isolated worktree and execution artifacts.
- `ddx-4ab66676` in the same attempt also surfaced `.git/index.lock` contention
  in the pre-execute checkpoint.
- `ddx-d0c656b0` at `20260506T160123-7b468ece` later failed review with
  `review-error: transport` and `claude quota-exhausted`.

Readiness verdict: mixed. Disk-space and stale-lock problems are pre-claim
checks. Provider quota exhaustion is only partially checkable because it

### Post-Attempt

hit `TestGraphQLPerfMatrix_Baseline` above budget. That is a post-attempt

Readiness verdict: no, not before claim. Bead-lifecycle can only detect this
after a targeted verification pass.

### Close-Policy

Representative failure: `ddx-8d747049` at `20260505T160236-8db426ba`.

commit or no_changes_rationale.txt`. That is not a code defect; it is a
closure-contract failure.

Readiness verdict: no. The only reliable detector is the post-attempt close
path, because the issue is about missing final evidence, not claim-time state.

### Unknown

Representative failure: `ddx-ff7c8ec9` at `20260505T012605-cd23d1fd`.
The reviewer emitted `review-error: unparseable JSON verdict: no JSON object
found`. The payload proves something went wrong, but not enough to classify it
as provider, host, bead readiness, or close-policy.

Readiness verdict: no. Unknowns remain a post-hoc investigation bucket until
the review contract or parser boundary records a more specific cause.

## Closing Notes

Documentation-only waiver: this report adds analysis only. No new Go test is
required because it does not add generated references or executable code.

The next improvement surface is the bead-lifecycle readiness and triage
taxonomy: local host checks, provider/reviewer transport classification, and

1:{"acceptance":"1. cli/internal/bead/axon/ exists with schema.graphql, query documents, genqlient config, and generated Go bindings.
2. TestAxonClient_SchemaBindingsCompile is added in cli/internal/bead/axon/ and exercises the generated package surface.
3. cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/axon/... passes.
4. lefthook run pre-commit passes.","created_at":"2026-05-05T02:41:06.667153148Z","description":"PROBLEM
TD-030 requires a typed Axon client package, but cli/internal/bead/axon_backend.go:20-26 still describes an in-process JSONL emulation. There is no cli/internal/bead/axon/ package yet, so the pinned schema snapshot, GraphQL operation documents, and genqlient output have nowhere to live.

ROOT CAUSE
cli/internal/bead/axon_backend.go:20-26 keeps the backend on the local JSONL-shaped emulation path, which means the repo currently has no package boundary for schema.graphql, query documents, or generated Go bindings. This leaves the TD-030 GraphQL-only client requirement unimplemented.

PROPOSED FIX
- Create cli/internal/bead/axon/schema.graphql as the pinned introspection snapshot for the Axon contract.
- Add query/mutation documents under cli/internal/bead/axon/queries/ for the bead CRUD surface needed by the package.
- Add genqlient config and generated Go bindings under cli/internal/bead/axon/ so the client can be compiled locally.
- Add a compile-focused test that instantiates the generated client surface and proves the bindings match the local package types.

NON-SCOPE
- WebSocket subscription transport.
- Replacing backend_axon.go with the wire implementation.
- Axon server-side schema or resolver work.

5. lefthook run pre-commit passes.","created_at":"2026-05-05T02:41:11.755929131Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":".execute-bead-wt-.execute-bead-wt-ddx-8d747049-20260505T023636-7669921a-b6bc9398-20260505T024017-db84a9cd-f2dae5c7","depends_on_id":".execute-bead-wt-.execute-bead-wt-ddx-8d747049-20260505T023636-7669921a-b6bc9398-20260505T024017-db84a9cd-a9e640e8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-05T02:41:18Z"}],"description":"PROBLEM
TD-030 requires a GraphQL subscription client for Axon changeEvents, but cli/internal/bead/axon_backend.go:20-26 still only documents a local JSONL emulation. There is no transport helper in cli/internal/bead/axon/ to open a live subscription stream against Axons WebSocket endpoint.

ROOT CAUSE
cli/internal/bead/axon_backend.go:20-26 keeps the backend in-process, so the package currently has no websocket transport boundary where GraphQL subscription setup, event decoding, and stream lifecycle management can live.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add a small subscription transport in cli/internal/bead/axon/ that opens a GraphQL WebSocket connection and subscribes to changeEvents.
- Define a narrow stream helper and event decoding path in the same package so the transport stays isolated from backend_axon.go.
- Add a focused subscription test that uses the helper to receive ordered changeEvents from a test endpoint.

NON-SCOPE
- Schema snapshot and genqlient binding generation.
- Replacing backend_axon.go with a production wire-path implementation.
- Axon server-side subscription schema work.

8:{"acceptance":"1. A reusable delimiter helper exists for untrusted artifact bodies.
2. Prompt assembly uses the helper at every artifact-body interpolation site in scope.
3. `TestArtifactBodyDelimitedAsUntrusted` proves the delimiter contract and payload preservation.
4. `cd cli && go test ./internal/server/... ./internal/agent/...` passes.
5. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.","created_at":"2026-05-05T11:37:14.708641486Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":".execute-bead-wt-ddx-0e5c3005-20260505T113434-3317d134-7fb75345","depends_on_id":"ddx-d6730314","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-06T03:30:30Z"}],"description":"PROBLEM
Prompt-injection boundary markers are not yet explicit around artifact bodies that later flow into prompt assembly. The current server-side artifact readers in `cli/internal/server/state_executions.go:205-217` clamp text for transport, but they do not wrap the body in an explicit untrusted-data envelope for downstream prompt renderers. The review prompt path in `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1561-1630` only uses XML tags for the bead envelope; it does not mark artifact content with a dedicated boundary contract.

ROOT CAUSE
There is no shared delimiter helper for untrusted artifact bodies. The current prompt builders rely on ad hoc XML wrappers and transport clamping, which are not the same thing as a prompt-injection boundary that clearly distinguishes trusted instructions from untrusted artifact text.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add a small helper (likely in `cli/internal/evidence` or the prompt-rendering package that owns the assembler) that wraps artifact bodies in a canonical untrusted-data delimiter pair.
- Update the prompt-rendering call sites that inline artifact bodies to use the wrapper before interpolation.
- Add a regression test named `TestArtifactBodyDelimitedAsUntrusted` that asserts the delimiter pair appears around the artifact payload and that the raw body is still preserved inside the wrapper.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not alter the transport-level clamp behavior or its `truncated` metadata.
- Do not change review verdict parsing or the FEAT-022 telemetry schema in this slice.
- Do not rewrite unrelated server text egress paths.","events":[{"actor":"ddx","body":"{"resolved_provider":"codex","resolved_model":"gpt-5.4-mini","fallback_chain":[],"actual_power":8}","created_at":"2026-05-05T14:44:37.174355984Z","kind":"routing","source":"ddx agent execute-bead","summary":"provider=codex model=gpt-5.4-mini"},{"actor":"ddx","body":"{"attempt_id":"20260505T143454-88ed54dc","harness":"codex","model":"gpt-5.4-mini","input_tokens":4861400,"output_tokens":17293,"total_tokens":4878693,"cost_usd":0,"duration_ms":580211,"exit_code":0}","created_at":"2026-05-05T14:44:37.425832645Z","kind":"cost","source":"ddx agent execute-bead","summary":"tokens=4878693 model=gpt-5.4-mini"},{"actor":"ddx","body":"{"escalation_count":0,"fallback_chain":[],"final_tier":"","requested_profile":"","requested_tier":"","resolved_model":"gpt-5.4-mini","resolved_provider":"codex","resolved_tier":""}","created_at":"2026-05-05T14:44:44.068530101Z","kind":"routing","source":"ddx agent execute-loop","summary":"provider=codex model=gpt-5.4-mini"},{"actor":"erik","body":"failure_class=review-error: transport

Scope:
- Lock contention: detect 'Unable to create .git/index.lock' / staging-tracker lock errors in attempt outcomes. Triage action: retry_with_backoff (e.g. 30s, 90s, 300s) within the same drain pass before unclaiming. Sibling bead handles the underlying lock fix; this bead wires the retry decision.
- execution_failed (substantive, not infrastructure): triage maps to escalate_tier per the existing S10 ladder; on exhaustion → needs_human. Distinguish from already-handled escalatable statuses by treating execution_failed as an explicit triage entry-point.
- no_changes (NoChangesRationale present): triage decides whether the bead AC is genuinely satisfied (close already_satisfied — current behavior) or whether to file a clarification follow-up bead (when rationale doesn't quote AC items).
- Tests for: lock-error → retry succeeds on second attempt; lock-error → escalate after backoff exhaustion; execution_failed → escalate_tier; no_changes with weak rationale → file follow-up.

Out of scope:
- The actual git-lock infrastructure fix (sibling bead).

The work described by this bead's AC was implemented and merged in commit
e188d9d3 ("feat(agent): wire triage dispatch for
lock_contention/execution_failed/no_changes [...]"), which is an ancestor of
the current execute-bead base 26d80c83. That commit added 755 lines across
three files:

  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_status.go    (+3)
  cli/internal/agent/triage_dispatch.go        (+405)
  cli/internal/agent/triage_dispatch_test.go   (+347)

AC mapping (all satisfied by code already present in the worktree):

  AC#1 — Lock-contention classification + retry-with-backoff with ≥3 steps
    before escalation:
    - IsLockContentionError (triage_dispatch.go) matches .git/index.lock,
      "another git process seems to be running", staging-tracker / tracker
      lock messages.
    - ClassifyAttemptForTriage routes execution_failed reports whose Detail
      matches IsLockContentionError to triage.FailureModeLockContention.
    - RunWithLockBackoff retries an AttemptFunc against a backoff schedule;
      LockContentionBackoff defaults to {30s, 90s, 300s} (three slots),
      sets Exhausted=true after consuming all slots so callers escalate.
    - DefaultPolicy.Ladders[FailureModeLockContention] =
      {retry_with_backoff, retry_with_backoff, needs_human} so cross-pass
      lock_contention also escalates.

  AC#2 — execution_failed enters triage and produces escalate_tier or
    needs_human depending on attempt count:
    - DefaultPolicy.Ladders[FailureModeExecutionFailed] =
      {re_attempt_with_context, escalate_tier, needs_human}.
    - DispatchPostAttempt records a triage-decision event per attempt;
      HistoryFromEvents recovers the prior-mode count so the ladder
      advances per occurrence (verified by
      TestDispatchExecutionFailedEscalates).

  AC#3 — no_changes with rationale not citing AC items files a clarification
    follow-up and closes the original:
    - RationaleCitesAC returns false on vague rationales and true when the
      rationale (a) is "specific" via rationaleIsSpecific (cites SHA, test
      name, file path) or (b) shares ≥2 AC content tokens of length ≥6.
    - DispatchPostAttempt, on ActionFileFollowup for FailureModeNoChanges,
      calls fileNoChangesFollowup which creates a child bead inheriting
      labels/spec-id, adds kind:clarification, embeds the worker's
      rationale and parent AC, and back-links via the
      triage_followup_for / triage_followup_reason Extra fields. Then
      CloseWithEvidence closes the parent (BaseRev as closing SHA).

  AC#4 — Tests cover all four scenarios:
    - TestRunWithLockBackoffSucceedsOnRetry — lock-error retry success
    - TestRunWithLockBackoffExhausts — backoff exhaustion across all three
      schedule slots
    - TestDispatchExecutionFailedEscalates — full execution_failed ladder
      (re_attempt → escalate_tier → needs_human, then clamps)
    - TestDispatchNoChangesFilesFollowup — weak-rationale no_changes files
      a clarification child and closes the parent
    Plus TestIsLockContentionError, TestClassifyAttemptForTriage,
    TestRationaleCitesAC, TestRunWithLockBackoffPropagatesNonLockError,
    TestRunWithLockBackoffRetriesOnAttemptError,
    TestDispatchNoChangesStrongRationaleSkipsTriage as supporting cases.

Verification:
  cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/ -run \
    'TestRunWithLockBackoff|TestDispatch|TestClassifyAttemptForTriage|TestIsLockContentionError|TestRationaleCitesAC' \
    -count=1
  → ok  github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/agent  0.024s

tests listed above. No further code change is required to satisfy the AC.

4. lefthook run pre-commit passes.","created_at":"2026-05-05T02:38:47.316272368Z","description":"PROBLEM
The Axon backend contract requires a generated GraphQL client plus a WebSocket subscription client, but the current implementation is an in-process JSONL emulation instead of a typed Axon package. The gap is visible in cli/internal/bead/axon_backend.go:15-26, which says the backend is a local emulation and not the wire-path implementation. There is no cli/internal/bead/axon/ package yet.

ROOT CAUSE
cli/internal/bead/axon_backend.go:20-26 explicitly keeps the backend as a JSONL-shaped emulation, so there is nowhere for genqlient-generated bindings, schema snapshots, query documents, or a subscription transport to live. The repo has no Axon client package to satisfy TD-030's GraphQL-only requirement.

PROPOSED FIX
- Create cli/internal/bead/axon/schema.graphql with the pinned introspection snapshot.
- Add query/mutation/subscription documents under cli/internal/bead/axon/queries/.
- Add genqlient config and generated Go bindings under cli/internal/bead/axon/.
- Add a small subscription transport in the same package for GraphQL WS changeEvents.

NON-SCOPE
- Replacing backend_axon.go itself; that is the follow-up implementation bead.
- Config routing and package-level test matrix changes.
- Axon server-side schema work.","events":[{"actor":"ddx","body":"{"resolved_provider":"codex","resolved_model":"gpt-5.4-mini","fallback_chain":[],"actual_power":8}","created_at":"2026-05-05T02:41:53.956840754Z","kind":"routing","source":"ddx agent execute-bead","summary":"provider=codex model=gpt-5.4-mini"},{"actor":"ddx","body":"{"attempt_id":"20260505T024017-db84a9cd","harness":"codex","model":"gpt-5.4-mini","input_tokens":873315,"output_tokens":10167,"total_tokens":883482,"cost_usd":0,"duration_ms":94607,"exit_code":0}","created_at":"2026-05-05T02:41:54.190399846Z","kind":"cost","source":"ddx agent execute-bead","summary":"tokens=883482 model=gpt-5.4-mini"},{"actor":"ddx","body":"{"escalation_count":0,"fallback_chain":[],"final_tier":"","requested_profile":"","requested_tier":"","resolved_model":"gpt-5.4-mini","resolved_provider":"codex","resolved_tier":""}","created_at":"2026-05-05T02:41:59.376323452Z","kind":"routing","source":"ddx agent execute-loop","summary":"provider=codex model=gpt-5.4-mini"},{"actor":"erik","body":"failure_class=review-error: transport
